rows.agency: DEPARTMENT OF SOCIAL SERVICES - RANDOLPH COUNTY
rows.service: Special Assistance offers a cash supplement to help pay for room and board in an adult care home or group home. Special Assistance In-Home offers a cash supplement to help people who are at risk of entering a residential facility but prefer to live at home. Long Term Care offers a cash supplement to help pay for room and board in a long term care or skilled nursing facility. Payments are made to the vendor
rows.eligibility: Meet income limits, live in an approved residential or skilled nursing facility, and have a disability or be age 65 and over. Must be a US citizen or eligible alien
rows.apply: Special Assistance Programs: Visit to complete an application or call to have it mailed. Long Term Care: Visit to complete an application, call to have it mailed, or apply online at epass.nc.gov (https://epass.nc.gov/)
rows.documents: Special Assistance: Last 3 years of bank statements, proof of household income and resources, proof of monthly expenses, and the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household. Long Term Care: Last 5 years of bank statements, an FL-2 form from a doctor, proof of household income and resources, proof of monthly expenses, and the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household
rows.notes: Site states Archdale satellite office at 215 Balfour Dr is in Archdale, not High Point; agency operates only in Randolph County.

rows.agency: DEPARTMENT OF SOCIAL SERVICES - RANDOLPH COUNTY
rows.service: Offers financial help with heating bills once every 12 months. Payments are based on household size, income, and heating source. Payments are made to the utility provider. People do not need to be in a crisis to get help
rows.eligibility: Meet income limits and resource guidelines. Each household is only eligible once, even if multiple members apply
rows.apply: Call to see if funds are available. Visit to complete an application, call to have 1 mailed, or apply online at epass.nc.gov (https://epass.nc.gov/). NOTE: Households with a person age 60 and over or with a person under age 60 who is receiving services from the NC Division of Aging and Adult Services can apply beginning December 1. All other households can apply beginning January 1
rows.documents: Photo ID, birth certificate, proof of household income, copy of utility bill, latest bank statement, the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household, and proof of disability if under age 60
rows.notes: Site lists 215 Balfour Drive as Archdale (not High Point) office; agency operates only in Randolph County.

rows.service: Offers emergency assistance for food, pet food, clothing, diapers, furniture, and household items. Offers emergency assistance for utilities including electric, gas, and water with disconnect notice. Offers financial help with ren with eviction notice, but only if the person applying can pay the rest of the amount due
rows.eligibility: Residents of coverage area
rows.apply: Visit the office or visit https://www.abccm.org/crisis-get-help (https://www.abccm.org/crisis-get-help) for more information
rows.documents: Food Pantry: Social Security Card, proof of current address, and photo ID. If first time for food, will not be turned away without Social Security Card. Rent/Utilities Assistance: Photo ID, proof of household income, Social Security Card and Shut-off or Eviction Notice. Proof of ability to pay majority of rent
rows.notes: Faith 360 disaster recovery program referenced on site.

rows.service: Connects disaster survivors in Western North Carolina with churches and community organizations to aid in long-term recovery. Offers information and referrals for housing, utility bills, food, clothing, transportation, home repairs, veteran services, and more
rows.eligibility: Disasters survivors in Western North Carolina
rows.apply: Call, email, or apply online at https://www.abccm.org/faith360-intake/ (https://www.abccm.org/faith360-intake/). A care coordinator will contact you within 48 hours after submitting the online application. If you are not military affiliated, select "prefer not to disclose."
rows.notes: Faith 360 disaster recovery program referenced on site.

Some people may be eligible if they have only worked in the area for the last 12 months, rather than lived in the area. Must have ability to pay a monthly mortgage, and pass a credit and criminal background check
rows.documents: Proof of household income and address
rows.notes: Homepage limited; could not verify specific details but site is operational.

rows.service: Sells donated clothes and household items like bedding, appliances, furniture, and more

rows.service: Offers financial help with Randolph Electric Membership Corporation utiity bills for people in a crisis
rows.eligibility: People in a crisis who need help with their Randolph Electric Membership Corporation bill. Each request is reviewed by the Board of Directors, and there is no guarantee of help. Examples of previously approved requests are: people with cancer, victims of a house fire, older adults on fixed incomes, and people dealing with unemployment
rows.documents: Proof of crisis and proof of income may be needed
rows.notes: Site lists Asheboro office zip 27205; phone 9109483401 is Robbins office, retained as 211 listed it; utility assistance program details not found on site.

rows.service: Offers a space to help with bicycle repair. Offers tools and other resources to help build or fix a bike

rows.service: Provides an "open closet" with needed baby equipment, clothing, and diapers and other items to families and parents in need to help care for their children
rows.schedule: By appointment only. Leave your contact information and they will return your call to take your order and set a date and time for pick up
rows.eligibility: Parents or families with children
rows.notes: Site confirms 48 Browndale Rd location; phone/hours not detailed.

rows.service: Offers coats in the winter. Coats are available for pick up at 23 Hamilton Street. After the main giveaway days, remaining coats will also be available throughout the winter
rows.eligibility: Open to all

rows.service: Offers donated items for babies. Items available may include baby clothes and shoes, diapers and diaper bags, baby bottles, books, toys, and more. Larger baby equipment like strollers and high chairs can be loaned out when available. Equipment is returned when outgrown. May also offer car seats for a small donation depending on availability. Limited supplies of formula or baby food may also be available. Visit website to find a location: bearcloset.org/locations (https://bearcloset.org/locations/)
rows.schedule: Location hours vary for each location. Visit website under "Our Locations" for more information
rows.eligibility: Anyone in need of baby equipment or clothing for children age 0 - 5
rows.notes: Site lists main phone 828-575-1140; 211 phone field was blank.

rows.service: Offers clothing and other personal items, furniture, appliances, and houseware
rows.eligibility: Open to all
rows.notes: Page references hours section but did not return detailed schedule data; address confirmed.

rows.service: Offers help to families with babies and young children by providing basic supplies they need. Most items are given for free, and some larger baby items are loaned at no cost and returned when no longer needed so another family can use them. Some locations may offer car seats for a small donation, and items may vary by location
rows.eligibility: Open to all
rows.notes: Site lists street as Sweeten Creek Road; 211 had typo Sweeten Cheek.

rows.service: Sells donated clothes and household items like bedding, appliances, furniture, and more

rows.eligibility: Open to all

rows.service: Sells donated clothes and household items like bedding, appliances, furniture, and more

rows.agency: JEWISH FAMILY SERVICES OF WESTERN NORTH CAROLINA
rows.service: Offers services and activities for older adults. Offers exercise classes, peer support, and more. Transportation may be available. Also offers caregiver respite, aging information, and resource referrals. Offers a congregate lunch meal for older adults
rows.eligibility: Older adults with limited ability to get out of the home or with a physical or memory limitation

rows.service: Offers refurbished cell phones, laptops, and essential digital connectivity services through partnerships with other Nonprofits, Government Agencies, & Schools to people in crisis
rows.eligibility: - People in Recovery and Transitional Housing - Men & Woman Returning from Incarceration - Families & Individuals living in Shelters - Children aging out of Foster Care & Adults w/ DSS Involvement - Survivors of Domestic Violence & Human Trafficking - Elderly and disabled members of the community
rows.notes: Site confirms refurbished phones/laptops for vulnerable populations through nonprofit partnerships.

rows.service: Offers a directory with information on housing, employment, health, in-home services, and wellness programs. Available online, and print copies are available as funds permit
rows.eligibility: Open to all
rows.apply: Call or visit website for more information
rows.notes: Site lists Mon-Fri 9am-4:30pm.

rows.service: Offers education and home improvements to reduce energy costs. Improvements may include sealing air leaks, adding insulation, fixing smoke and carbon monoxide detectors, and more
rows.eligibility: Homeowners or renters with landlord permission who meet income limits
rows.apply: Call or visit website to download application. Email completed application
rows.documents: Proof of household income, electric/heating fuel bills for the last 12 months, and proof of homeownership or a permission letter from landlord

rows.service: Offers affordable rental homes and apartments. Properties owned by MHO are managed by Partnership Property Management, who processes all applications and handles availability of apartments. They have property managers located at most of the apartment complexes owned by MHO, Inc. in Buncombe and the surrounding counties
rows.eligibility: Have good rental record and credit score. Must meet income limits
rows.apply: Call or visit website for apartment listings
rows.documents: Photo ID and proof of income
rows.notes: Site confirms Asheville HQ at 53 S French Broad Ave Ste 100, Buncombe County; 211 had Greensboro address (incorrect agency location).

rows.service: Offers temporary emergency shelter to people who are facing homelessness because of intimate partner abuse. Offers emergency food, clothing, and toiletry items to shelter residents
rows.eligibility: People who are actively fleeing an intimate partner violence relationship
rows.notes: Site shows operations from Buncombe County Family Justice Center at 35 Woodfin St; shelter location confidential.

rows.service: Offers overnight shelter when temperatures are freezing or severe. The Asheville-Buncombe Homeless Coalition will call for Code Purple when the shelter is activated. Bus fare is continuing to be free to and from the shelter
rows.schedule: Entry: 6:00pm - 8:00pm when Code Purple is called. Shelter available until 7:30am
rows.eligibility: Single women or women with children experiencing homelessness
rows.apply: Visit website to see if Code Purple has been called. It will also be announced on local media channels. Code Purple must be called by at least 12:00pm the day before it's activated so the shelter can prepare
rows.notes: Code Purple shelter at VRQ (men) referenced; specific entry hours not on homepage.

rows.service: Offers overnight shelter when temperatures are freezing or severe. The Asheville-Buncombe Homeless Coalition will call for Code Purple when the shelter is activated. Bus fare is continuing to be free to and from the shelter
rows.schedule: Entry: 6:00pm - 8:00pm when Code Purple is called. Shelter available until 7:00am
rows.eligibility: Men experiencing homelessness
rows.apply: Visit website to see if Code Purple has been called. It will also be announced on local media channels. Code Purple must be called by at least 12:00pm the day before it's activated so the shelter can prepare
rows.notes: Code Purple shelter at VRQ (men) referenced; specific entry hours not on homepage.

rows.service: Offers overnight shelter when temperatures are freezing or severe. The Asheville-Buncombe Homeless Coalition will call for Code Purple when the shelter is activated
rows.schedule: Entry is open from 6:00pm - 8:00pm when Code Purple is called. Law enforcement and paramedic escorts are also available after these hours. Entry after 8:00p.m must be facilitated by public safety or outreach staff
rows.eligibility: Men experiencing homelessness
rows.apply: Call to see if Code Purple has been called. It will also be announced on local media channels. Code Purple must be called by at least 12:00pm the day before it's activated so the shelter can prepare
rows.notes: Old URL redirects to Haywood Rd Corps page; specific Code Purple emergency shelter details not on this corps page.

rows.service: Offers a directory to search for local farms, farmers markets, and businesses. Search the directory here: https://appalachiangrown.org/ (https://appalachiangrown.org/)
rows.eligibility: Open to all

rows.service: Offers a food pantry, hot meals and food banks
rows.eligibility: Open to all

rows.service: Offers emergency help for food, pet food, clothing, diapers, furniture and household items. Helps with heating fuel when income and resources are not enough to meet basic needs. Offers emergency assistance for utilities, including electric, gas, and water with a disconnect notice, and rent with an eviction notice
rows.eligibility: Residents of North Buncombe County
rows.apply: Visit the office or visit https://www.abccm.org/crisis-get-help (https://www.abccm.org/crisis-get-help) for more information
rows.documents: Food Pantry: Social Security Card, proof of current address, and Photo ID. If first time for food, will not be turned away without Social Security Card. Rent/Utilities Assistance: Photo ID, proof of household income, Social Security Card and Shut-off or Eviction Notice
rows.notes: Faith 360 disaster recovery program referenced on site.

rows.service: Offers emergency help for food, pet food, clothing, diapers, furniture and household items. Helps with heating fuel when income and resources are not enough to meet the basic needs. Offers emergency assistance for utilities, including electric, gas, and water with a disconnect notice, and rent with a late notice from landlord or eviction notice
rows.eligibility: Residents within the coverage area. Must have a utility disconnect notice or eviction notice for help with utilities or rent
rows.apply: Visit the office or visit "https://www.abccm.org/crisis-get-help" for more information
rows.documents: Food Pantry: Social Security Card, proof of current address, and photo ID. If first time for food, will not be turned away without Social Security Card. Rent/Utilities Assistance: Photo ID, proof of household income, Social Security card, and disconnect notice, late notice from landlord, or eviction notice
rows.notes: Faith 360 disaster recovery program referenced on site.

rows.service: Offers critical necessities like food, water, and healthcare for survival and stability. Offers warm and safe housing for the displaced and homeless. Offers training and employment related opportunities. Provides services for seniors and disabled veterans

rows.eligibility: Open to all
rows.apply: Call or visit website for more information
rows.notes: Site advises calling/emailing as staff are often in field; office hours not posted.

rows.service: Offers a food pantry. Also offers pet food, adult and baby diapers, toiletries, and feminine hygiene products
rows.schedule: Food Pantry Hours: Wednesday, 11:00am - 1:00pm. Office Hours: Monday - Thursday, 9:30am - 4:00pm
rows.eligibility: Open to all
rows.apply: Call to schedule an appointment. Can schedule up to 1 week in advance

rows.agency: CHILDREN FIRST/COMMUNITIES IN SCHOOLS - BUNCOMBE COUNTY
rows.service: Offers tutoring, mentoring, and case management. Also offers group support for social skills and bullying prevention. Offers food, hygiene products, clothing, and emergency financial assistance. Also offers school supplies during the school year, if available. Also offers information and referrals to other community resources
rows.schedule: Hours vary. Call for more information
rows.eligibility: Families with a child attending one of the following schools: Claxton, Emma, and Estes Elementary, and Eblen Intermediate. Some programs may have more specific eligibility requirements

rows.agency: CHILDREN FIRST/COMMUNITIES IN SCHOOLS - BUNCOMBE COUNTY
rows.service: Offers a food pantry, parenting classes, and more. Also offers help with SNAP applications and limited emergency financial assistance
rows.eligibility: Families of students at Emma or Estes Elementary Schools

rows.service: Offers a food pantry, fresh produce, clothes and essentials. Also offers a hot community lunch

rows.eligibility: Open to all
rows.apply: Food Pantry: Visit. Community Meal: Call to verify dates hot meals will be served for the month
rows.notes: Site homepage didn't list pantry hours; address and phone match 211.

rows.service: Offers a sit-in, hot breakfast and pre-boxed food. Also offers men, women, and children's clothing once a month. Also offers showers once a week
rows.eligibility: Open to all. Food boxes and Clothing closets are limited to once a month
rows.documents: Vary, depending on service. Photo ID required for most services. Social Security Number required for some services

rows.service: Food is available at community markets in different locations throughout the county. Markets also offer connections to agencies that can help with chronic disease management and prevention, finding a job, and more. Visit website to find closest community market: https://www.buncombecounty.org/governing/depts/community-engagement/default.aspx#sec-markets-connectors (https://www.buncombecounty.org/governing/depts/community-engagement/default.aspx#sec-markets-connectors)
rows.eligibility: Open to all
rows.apply: Visit website to find next market. Visit the market

rows.eligibility: Open to all

rows.service: Offers food and personal care items to people living with HIV/AIDS, or people receiving home hospice care with any diagnosis

rows.apply: Referral from another agency such as WNCCHS or WNCAP, doctor's office, Buncombe County Health and Human Services, CarePartners, or Four Seasons Hospice. Call or email for more information. Delivery available to people who are homebound, living within Buncombe County

rows.service: Offers emergency food and also offers clothing when available. Also offers diapers and pet food when available
rows.eligibility: May receive food once every 30 days. May receive clothing once every 90 days. TEFAP is available once a week as supplies last

rows.service: Offers food boxes

rows.schedule: Mobile pantry hours and locations vary
rows.eligibility: Open to all

rows.agency: NORTH CAROLINA COOPERATIVE EXTENSION CENTER - BUNCOMBE COUNTY
rows.service: Offers forestry management services and agricultural production information. Offers nutrition information workshops and food safety education. Also offers youth programs such as 4-H activities and clubs
rows.eligibility: Open to all. Youth programs available for children and adolescents, age 5 - 19. Other programs may have more specific eligibility requirements
rows.apply: Visit, call, or visit website to learn more about programs. Some may require registration

rows.service: Offers emergency financial help for people with intellectual or developmental disabilities and their families. Able to help with utilities, housing, groceries, medical expenses, transportation, and clothing. Also offers summer camp scholarships
rows.eligibility: Someone in the home must have an intellectual or developmental disability. Must have contacted other agencies (ABCCM, Eblen, St. Vincent de Paul, etc.) for help already. Must have an eviction notice or utility disconnection notice. Need must be related to Hurricane Helene
rows.apply: Call. Applications for summer camp scholarships begins in March. Call for more information
rows.notes: Address with Suite 246; hours not listed on website.

rows.service: Coordinates prevention, treatment, and support services for people with intellectual/developmental disabilities, mental health issues, or substance use needs. Offers Supported Employment services for people with intellectual/developmental disabilities help find competitive integrated employment or self-employment. Offers a 24-hour Crisis Line for people looking for services or in a mental health crisis. Also offers drop-in crisis services. Find locations here:https://www.vayahealth.com/get-help/walk-crisis-centers/ (https://www.vayahealth.com/get-help/walk-crisis-centers/) Offers a Customer Service line to help with a concern or complaint. Also accepts reports of Medicaid fraud
rows.schedule: Crisis Line: 24 hours a day. Member Service Line: Monday - Saturday, 7:00am - 6:00pm. Fraud Reporting: 24 hours a day. Administrative Office: Monday - Friday, 8:30am - 5:00pm
rows.eligibility: Crisis Services: Anyone having a mental health, substance use, or developmental disability crisis, regardless of insurance. Non-crisis Services: Uninsured people and people with Medicaid
rows.apply: General Services: Call or visit. File a Concern/Complaint: Call the Member Service Line or email member.services@vayahealth.com. Report Fraud: Call, email legalandcompliance@vayahealth.com, or visit vayahealth.ethicspoint.com (https://vayahealth.ethicspoint.com/l)
rows.documents: Give name, phone number, and date of birth when calling
rows.notes: Site confirms member services 1-800-962-9003 Mon-Sat 7am-6pm; behavioral health crisis line 1-800-849-6127 24/7.

rows.service: Offers bilingual services, supporting BIPOC and LGBTQ+ communities. Provides affordable housing assistance, home repairs, and debris removal
rows.schedule: Hours vary, call for more information
rows.eligibility: Housing Assistance: Must be homeowners and fall at or below 80% AMI. Other(s): Call for more information
rows.notes: Site confirms home repair phone 828-276-3544, matches 211; address not specified for housing program.

rows.agency: WESTERN NORTH CAROLINA COMMUNITY HEALTH SERVICES
rows.service: Offers primary healthcare services. Offers screenings and treatment of acute and chronic illnesses. Offers general physical examinations. Offers immunizations and vaccines, including some for foreign travel. Minnie Jones Health Center also offers baby diapers
rows.eligibility: Open to all
rows.apply: Call or visit the office to schedule an appointment
rows.documents: Photo ID. Insurance card if available or proof of income for sliding scale

rows.service: Delivers shelf-stable food boxes to homebound older adults
rows.eligibility: Homebound, age 60 and over, meet income limits, and have no access to transportation
rows.apply: Call to schedule a home visit and assessment. Delivers food once a month
rows.notes: Site lists Mon-Fri 9am-4:30pm.

rows.service: Offers home repairs. Offers painting/staining, roof repair, heating system repair, and more. Also offers modifications for accessibility, such as wheelchair ramps or bathroom modifications. Participants make low monthly payments on a short-term loan
rows.eligibility: Meet income limits and have owned and lived in the home for at least 1 year. Veterans encouraged to apply. For a mobile home, must also own the land under the mobile home
rows.documents: Proof of income and home ownership

rows.service: Offers home repairs for people whose homes are a risk to their health and safety. Repairs can be for ramps, stairs, handrails, furnace, roof, and plumbing
rows.eligibility: Meet income limits
rows.apply: Call, visit the office, or apply online
rows.documents: Proof of address and proof of income

rows.service: Offers home repairs due to Hurricane Helene
rows.eligibility: Meet income limits
rows.documents: Proof of income and home ownership

rows.agency: ONTRACK WNC FINANCIAL EDUCATION AND COUNSELING
rows.service: Offers an in-person workshop offering an overview of the homebuying process. Workshop is required for many down payment and affordable housing programs
rows.schedule: Tue 9:00am - 4:00pm; Wed 9:00am - 4:00pm; Thu 9:00am - 4:00pm; Mon, Fri, evenings by appointment
rows.eligibility: Open to all
rows.apply: Register for a class on the website
rows.notes: Site states office hours Tue-Thu 9am-4pm with Mon, Fri, and evenings by appointment only.

rows.service: Sells donated furniture, home goods, building materials, and appliances
rows.schedule: Asheville ReStore: Tuesday - Saturday, 9:00am - 6:00pm. Weaverville ReStore: Tuesday - Saturday, 10:00am - 6:00pm
rows.eligibility: Open to all

rows.agency: HOMEWARD BOUND OF WESTERN NORTH CAROLINA
rows.service: Offers permanent supportive housing and intense case management to vulnerable populations
rows.eligibility: People with disabilities who are experiencing chronic homelessness
rows.apply: Call to complete an intake screening
rows.documents: None for intake screening. More documents may be needed later

rows.service: Providing lighting options for survivors who are in need of free lamps for rebuilt/repaired homes of Helene survivors
rows.eligibility: Survivors of Hurricane Helene
rows.notes: No website on file; could not verify against agency site.

rows.service: Offers rental properties with rent based on income
rows.eligibility: Meet income limits and complete a background check
rows.apply: Apply online only. Call or visit website for waitlist information. Disabled may call, email or visit Monday - Wednesday to complete an application
rows.documents: Photo ID, Social Security Card, birth certificate, and proof of income

rows.service: Offers loans to cover the down payments or closing costs when purchasing a home. Program participants must complete an OnTrack Financial Education and Counseling Services homebuyer education course before the close of the loan
rows.eligibility: Be a first time homeowner and meet income limits. Must have 1 year of steady employment, be able to contribute own funds, have good credit score, and have lived in Western North Carolina for at least 1 year
rows.apply: Call or visit the website for application. Email or fax completed application

rows.service: Offers financial help for laundry services at a local laundromat. Also offers laundry supplies. May be able to offer transportation to laundromat if needed
rows.eligibility: Open to people experiencing homelessness, people who meet income limits, and others who do not have access to laundry facilities
rows.notes: National Laundry Love site does not contain Asheville-specific schedule or phone.

rows.agency: DEPARTMENT OF SOCIAL SERVICES - BUNCOMBE COUNTY
rows.service: Special Assistance offers a cash supplement to help pay for room and board in an adult care home or group home. Long Term Care offers a cash supplement to help pay for room and board in a long term care or skilled nursing facility. Payments are made to the vendor
rows.eligibility: Meet income limits, live in an approved residential or skilled nursing facility, and have a disability or be age 65 and over
rows.apply: Special Assistance: Visit to complete an application or call to have 1 mailed. Long Term Care: Visit to complete an application, call to have 1 mailed, or apply online at epass.nc.gov (https://epass.nc.gov/)
rows.documents: Special Assistance: Last 3 years of bank statements, proof of household income and resources, proof of monthly expenses, and the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household. Long Term Care: Last 5 years of bank statements, an FL-2 form from a doctor, proof of household income and resources, proof of monthly expenses, and the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household
rows.notes: Site redirects to buncombenc.gov.

rows.service: Offers an interactive search map to help look for affordable housing. The map can filter for different needs such as income, apartment size, and more: Affordable Housing Map (https://bunco.maps.arcgis.com/apps/webappviewer/index.htmlid=99ad0aa435124d3e98c776c5b6e6f33c)
rows.eligibility: Open to all. Some apartment may have more specific eligibility for age and disability
rows.notes: Site redirects to buncombenc.gov; affordable housing map referenced.

rows.service: Oversees administrative and government services. Board of Elections : Offers election information and voter registration. Register of Deeds : Issues land deeds, marriage licenses, and birth and death certificates. Tax Department : Collects property taxes. People may be eligible for tax payment relief or exemption if they meet income limits and are one of the following: age 65 and over, under age 65 with a disability, a veteran, or a widow of a veteran who has not remarried. Register of Deeds office also offers baby diapers
rows.eligibility: Open to all
rows.documents: Vary, depending on service. Call for more information
rows.notes: Site redirects to buncombenc.gov; specific Register of Deeds details not on homepage.

rows.agency: MEALS ON WHEELS - ASHEVILLE AND BUNCOMBE COUNTY
rows.service: Delivers meals to homebound older adults. Pet food is also available for clients
rows.eligibility: Homebound, age 60 and over, and unable to drive or attend a congregate lunch site
rows.apply: Call or email to start the intake process

rows.agency: DEPARTMENT OF SOCIAL SERVICES - BUNCOMBE COUNTY
rows.service: Offers health insurance for people with low income and limited resources. Medicaid transportation is available to Medicaid recipients who need assistance getting to Medicaid-approved medical appointments and providers
rows.eligibility: Medicaid : Eligibility based on age, pregnancy status, disability status, income, and resources. Medicaid Transportation: People already receiving Medicaid through Medicaid Direct or the Eastern Band of Cherokee Indians tribal health plan. People already receiving Medicaid through a different health plan (AmeriHealth Caritas, Carolina Complete Health, HealthyBlue, UnitedHealthcare, or WellCare) must contact their plan provider for transportation
rows.apply: Medicaid : Visit to complete an application, call to have 1 mailed, or apply online at epass.nc.gov (https://epass.nc.gov/). Medicaid Transportation: Call to schedule medical transportation
rows.documents: Proof of household income and resources, proof of monthly expenses, and the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household
rows.notes: Site redirects to buncombenc.gov; specific DSS page not directly accessible.

rows.program: Medical Respite Facilities/Beds for Homeless People
rows.service: Offers a safe place for adults experiencing homelessness to rest, get 3 meals a day, and be helped in other ways following discharge from a hospital after surgery or acute illness
rows.schedule: 24/7 Facility. Guest hours limited. Call ahead
rows.eligibility: People experiencing homelessness recently discharged from a hospital after surgery or acute illness. Must be independent with activities of daily living. Typical length of stay at Haywood Street Respite is 2 weeks
rows.apply: Call or apply online. A health care professional will be needed to complete referral paperwork

rows.service: Offers transportation to medical appointments and other destinations for older adults
rows.eligibility: Age 60 and over
rows.apply: Call to schedule a ride. Rides must be scheduled at least 7 days in advance and are based on volunteer availability

rows.service: Offers transportation to medical appointments and job interviews within Buncombe County
rows.eligibility: Open to all. Must be a resident of Buncombe County
rows.documents: Photo ID and proof of appointment

rows.service: Offers low-income families with opportunities for homeownership through financing and construction of affordable homes. Must complete homeownership education classes offered by OnTrack Financial Education and Counseling
rows.eligibility: Meet income limits, have stable employment and credit, and have lived or worked in Western North Carolina for at least 1 year
rows.apply: Call or visit website to download application

rows.service: Offers home mortgage and mortgage refinancing loans to people unable to use traditional banking services. For location information and phone numbers, visit https://www.self-help.org/locations/nc-va-locations (https://www.self-help.org/locations/nc-va-locations)
rows.eligibility: Open to all
rows.apply: Visit, apply online, or visit website to print and complete application to become a member. Once a member, call or visit for loan information
rows.documents: Photo ID and Social Security card
rows.notes: Page returned 403 forbidden; could not verify.

rows.service: Offers support and help for veterans at risk for, or are experiencing homelessness. Support can be immediate food and shelter, job training, healthcare, and financial support to prevent homelessness
rows.eligibility: Veteran experiencing homelessness or at risk of becoming homeless
rows.notes: Site at va.gov/asheville-health-care; address 1100 Tunnel Road confirmed.

rows.agency: HOMEWARD BOUND OF WESTERN NORTH CAROLINA
rows.service: Offers a homeless resource center and day shelter. Offers showers, phones, mail services, and hygiene items. Offers information and referrals to community resources and help getting identifying documents. Diapers may also be available
rows.eligibility: People experiencing homelessness. Pets allowed

rows.service: Offers education and home improvements to reduce energy costs. Improvements may include sealing air leaks, adding insulation, water heater and heat pump replacement, switching out light bulbs, health and safety home repairs and more. Can offer further help if needed
rows.eligibility: People living in single family homes or mobile homes who meet income limits, 200% of Federal Poverty Level. Can be renters or owners. Unable to help people living in multi-unit apartment building. Energy Savers is a non-profit Duke Energy Qualified weatherization service provider
rows.documents: Proof of household income and electric bill
rows.notes: Site only lists PO Box 142, Asheville 28802 mailing; 211 street address (Stegall Lane) not on site but kept.

rows.service: Offers bus service, with schedules that vary by route, throughout the City of Asheville and other local areas. Find real-time route information by texting "NextART" followed by the bus stop ID to 41411, calling 828-259-5943 , or visiting https://www.ashevillenc.gov/service/transit-maps-schedules/ (https://www.ashevillenc.gov/service/transit-maps-schedules/)
rows.schedule: Mon 6:00am - 9:30pm; Tue 6:00am - 9:30pm; Wed 6:00am - 9:30pm; Thu 6:00am - 9:30pm; Fri 6:00am - 9:30pm; Sat 7:00am - 9:30pm; Sun 8:30am - 6:00pm;
rows.eligibility: Open to all
rows.apply: Call or visit website to find route information
rows.documents: None for general riders. Discounted riders may need to provide other documents. Call for more information

rows.service: Offers bus service, with schedules that vary by route. Find real-time route information by visiting: www.buncombecounty.org/governing/depts/transportation/trailblazer-routes.aspx (http://www.buncombecounty.org/governing/depts/transportation/trailblazer-routes.aspx)
rows.schedule: Black Mountain Trailblazer: Monday - Saturday, 5:50am - 7:45pm. Enka-Candler Trailblazer: Monday - Friday, 6:15am - 6:10pm. North Buncombe Trailblazer: Monday - Friday, 6:30am - 6:30pm
rows.eligibility: Open to all
rows.apply: Call or visit website to find route information
rows.notes: Site redirects to buncombenc.gov/MountainMobility; Trailblazer routes referenced.

rows.service: Offers books, large print books, and audio books. May also offer videos, music CDs, and art prints. Most library materials can be reserved and renewed online. Offers free internet access and Wi-Fi. Also offers computer classes. Visit https://www.buncombecounty.org/governing/depts/library/ (https://www.buncombecounty.org/governing/depts/library/)to find local library. Pack Memorial Library also offers baby diapers
rows.schedule: Hours vary. Visit website for more information
rows.eligibility: Open to all. Materials can be checked out with a library card
rows.documents: Photo ID may be needed for library card
rows.notes: Site redirected to buncombenc.gov; Pack Memorial address confirmed and phone added; site lists 13 branches with varying hours.

rows.agency: HOMEWARD BOUND OF WESTERN NORTH CAROLINA
rows.service: Offers services to homeless veterans and their families. Offers outreach, case management, and help getting VA and other public benefits. Also offers short-term financial help with rent and rental deposits
rows.eligibility: Veterans experiencing homelessness who meet income limits. Unable to help veterans who have a dishonorable discharge
rows.documents: Photo ID, Social Security card, DD 214 paperwork, proof of income, and proof of homelessness

rows.agency: HOMEWARD BOUND OF WESTERN NORTH CAROLINA
rows.service: Offers rapid rehousing and supportive services for people experiencing homelessness. Services may include financial help with rent or rental deposits, case management, and more. Services are available for up to 2 years
rows.eligibility: People experiencing homelessness who meet income limits. Referral has to be made through a homeless shelter or transitional living facility, such as AHope Day Center, Salvation Army, Safe Shelter, Eliada Home or Transformation Village
rows.apply: Call to see if funds are available. Visit to complete an assessment. There is a screening process. Referrals made through a coordinated assessment meeting

rows.service: Helps families build their own homes under the guidance of a construction supervisor. Families contribute "sweat equity" toward the purchase of their home. Homes are financed with USDA Direct Loan construction financing
rows.eligibility: Meet income limits, have acceptable credit, and at least 1 year of stable employment. Willing to contribute "sweat equity" volunteer hours
rows.apply: Call or visit website to download application

rows.service: Offers services to homeless veterans and their families, or veterans at risk of losing their housing. Offers services including outreach, case management, rapid rehousing, homelessness prevention, service coordination, employment and training, and help getting VA and other public benefits. Offers short-term financial help with rent and rental deposits when funds are available
rows.eligibility: Veterans who have served at least 1 active day and were discharged in a character of service other than dishonorable who meet income limits. Experiencing homelessness or at imminent risk of being homeless
rows.documents: Photo ID, DD 214 documentation, proof of household income and if applicable, an eviction notice
rows.notes: SSVF veterans rent assistance referenced; specific phone not on homepage.

rows.service: Offers financial help with rent and utilities. Payment goes directly to vendor. Also offers assistance with gasoline and eyeglasses
rows.eligibility: Rental and Utility Assistance: Meet income limits and be experiencing a crisis or hardship. There is a maximum amount of times assistance can be received. Gasoline Assistance: Have a medical appointment, job interview, or start date for a new job
rows.apply: Rental and Utility Assistance: Visit website to schedule appointment, download application forms, and return via email along with required documents at least 3 days before the appointment date. If no appointments are available, and you are facing eviction or utility disconnection , call the office. Eyeglasses and Gasoline Assistance: Apply in person
rows.documents: Rental Assistance: Photo ID, official copy of eviction notice/court documents, copy of lease agreement, and proof of income or lack of income. Utility Assistance: Photo ID, proof of income or lack of income, and award letters of other forms of assistance, if available. Gasoline Assistance: Photo ID, proof of medical appointment, job interview, or new hire

rows.service: Offers limited rental and utility assistance. Sometimes bus tickets or laundry vouchers may be distributed
rows.eligibility: Open to all
rows.apply: Call, visit the office, or apply online
rows.notes: Church homepage has no information on rental/utility assistance program.

rows.service: Offers financial assistance for rent, utilities and mortgages. Funding limited. Call to check on available funds. Offers financial assistance and emergency housing ONLY for those suffering economic hardship from Hurricane Helene
rows.schedule: Hours vary, call for more information
rows.eligibility: Open to all. Emergency Housing - Only for those impacted by Hurricane Helene
rows.apply: Email rentsupport@gcpcusa.org. Do not call church for assistance requests.
rows.notes: Site directs assistance requests via email rentsupport@gcpcusa.org only - explicitly says do not call.

rows.agency: HOMEWARD BOUND OF WESTERN NORTH CAROLINA
rows.service: Offers financial help with rent and case management to people who have experienced homelessness in the past and currently have an eviction notice. May also offer limited financial help with utilities
rows.eligibility: People who have experienced homelessness in the last 7 years, have a current eviction notice, and meet income limits. Priority will go to people most at risk of becoming homeless again
rows.documents: Proof of income and eviction notice

rows.service: Offers financial help for rent and utilities such as electric, gas, and water. Also helps with utility deposits
rows.eligibility: Residents of North and West Buncombe County and Central Asheville (North of I-40) who meet income limits. Able to accept about 5 clients a week
rows.apply: Call Monday morning to leave a voice mail with name, number and reason for request. Must be available on Monday to receive a call back. Operates on a week-to-week basis. No same day assistance
rows.notes: No website on file; could not verify against agency site.

rows.service: Offers a one time assistance housing support grant to assist members or recipients with up to $500 for a rental deposit, utility deposit or first month's rent to get into housing. Does not assist with past-due rent or utilities
rows.eligibility: Must have Medicaid insurance, specifically with a Vaya Health tailored care plan. This program requires a referral process from a contracted provider with Vaya Health. The contracted provider must be willing to complete the paperwork with the member or recipient. Contact provider for eligibility. If approved, payment will be sent directly to the landlord or utility company. After paperwork is received it takes up to two weeks for finance to send payment to the landlord/utility company once approved
rows.documents: Work with your provider to complete Vaya Health's One Time Assistance Housing Support Grant referral paperwork
rows.notes: Homepage does not detail One Time Assistance Housing Support Grant program or its specific contact info.

rows.service: Provides low/no-cost access to tools, education, repair services, and other resources. Repairs consist of tuning and sharpening chainsaws, repairing generators, and other items needed for survival, cleaning, and rebuilding
rows.eligibility: Open to all

rows.service: Offers transportation for grocery shopping, retail shopping, and non-medical appointments
rows.schedule: Service Hours: Monday - Saturday, 5:30am - 7:30pm. Office Hours: Monday - Friday, 8:30am - 4:30pm
rows.eligibility: Call for eligibility
rows.apply: Call for eligibility. Trips must be scheduled at least 1 day in advance
rows.notes: Site redirects to buncombenc.gov/MountainMobility; address and phone confirmed.

rows.service: Offers assistance to voucher holders to find rental units in the private market
rows.notes: Site confirms landlord-tenant partnership with HACA; PO Box 2464 Asheville 28802, no phone listed.

rows.service: Offers transitional housing and emergency shelter for veterans
rows.schedule: Residents must arrive by 4:00pm. Check-in is 4:30pm and check-out is at 8:00am. Shelter staff available 24 hours a day
rows.eligibility: Veteran men age 18 and over
rows.apply: Call to check availability and be added to the waiting list
rows.notes: Steadfast House women's shelter at 30 Olin Haven Way referenced on site.

rows.service: Offers transitional housing and an emergency shelter for women and children
rows.eligibility: Single women and mothers with children experiencing homelessness
rows.apply: Call to be placed on waitlist. Call back every Tuesday to remain on the waitlist. Include the following information in message: contact information, current living situation, and age and number of children if applicable. Female veteran's should call the veteran's waitlist phone number
rows.notes: Steadfast House women's shelter at 30 Olin Haven Way referenced on site.

rows.service: Offers an emergency overnight shelter for homeless men. Offers Christian-based case management and support services to residents who commit to personal recovery plan. Includes help with developing skills, getting medical care, and finding a job
rows.schedule: Intake: Monday - Thursday, 9:00am - 11:00am. Shelter: Monday - Sunday, 4:00pm - 7:00am
rows.eligibility: Men age 18 and over without children. Limited to 3 nights stay without a rescue plan. Cannot accept sex offenders
rows.apply: Visit during intake hours. Beds filled on a first-come, first-served basis. Call for bed availability

rows.service: Offers a lunch. May also offer personal hygiene items and clothing
rows.eligibility: Open to all
rows.notes: Soup kitchen at 1543 Patton Ave; specific hours/phone for this location not posted on homepage.

rows.service: Offers a hot meal, shoe closet and personal hygiene products on Sundays after church
rows.eligibility: Homeless and those in need
rows.notes: Site lists Sunday schedule: 1pm coffee/snacks, 1:30pm worship, 2pm lunch.

rows.service: Offers locally sourced hot meals distributed daily to public housing and underserved populations
rows.schedule: Hours vary, call for more information
rows.eligibility: Call to see if eligible
rows.apply: Call or visit website for more information

rows.service: Offers a drop-in day center during the winter months. Offers hot coffee and a meal
rows.eligibility: Open to all. Program runs from November to March
rows.notes: Saturday Sanctuary mentioned; specific hours not on homepage but address/phone confirmed.

rows.service: Offers a home-cooked meal served family-style
rows.eligibility: Open to all
rows.apply: Visit. Call ahead for a large group

rows.service: Offers a hot breakfast and snacks to go. Clothing, blankets, and sleeping bags may also be available
rows.eligibility: Open to all
rows.notes: Site states meal served at 9am Saturday; 211 had 8:30am start (which is prep time per site).

rows.service: Offers a free restaurant-style breakfast meal
rows.eligibility: Open to all
rows.notes: Facebook page content truncated/inaccessible via WebFetch; kept 211 values.

rows.service: Offers lunch and dinner meals
rows.schedule: Lunch: Monday, Tuesday, Thursday, Friday, 12:00pm. Dinner: Monday - Sunday, 4:30pm
rows.eligibility: Open to all

rows.service: Local volunteers work with eligible families to build homes. Qualified homebuyers pay back a mortgage over 30 years. Offers a special program called Aging in Place for older adults to buy a home. Homes are designed with ramps and wheelchair-accessible doorways
rows.eligibility: Regular Homeownership: Meet income limits, have lived in the area for the last 12 months, and are willing to contribute "sweat equity" volunteer hours. Some people may be eligible if they have only worked in the area for the last 12 months, rather than lived in the area. Must have ability to pay a monthly mortgage, and pass a credit and criminal background check. Aging in Place Program: Age 55 and over, meet income limits, have lived in the area for the last 12 months, and are willing to contribute "sweat equity" volunteer hours
rows.apply: Regular Homeownership: Attend an optional 1-hour information session and complete an application. Visit https://www.ashevillehabitat.org/housing-programs/home-ownership/ (https://www.ashevillehabitat.org/housing-programs/home-ownership/) or call for upcoming information session dates. Aging in Place Program: Attend an optional 1-hour information session and complete an application. Visit https://www.ashevillehabitat.org/housing-programs/home-ownership/aging-in-place/ (https://www.ashevillehabitat.org/housing-programs/home-ownership/aging-in-place/) or call for upcoming information session dates
rows.documents: Proof of household income and address

rows.service: Offers property tax relief to seniors, those who are totally & permanently disabled; and for Veterans with a permanent and total service-connected disability
rows.eligibility: Seniors (65+), People who are disabled, or fully disabled Veterans
rows.notes: Senior/Disability Assistance phone 828-250-4915 confirmed; Tax Assessment Office at 182 College St.

rows.service: Offers transitional housing for homeless men, women, and children. Residents are offered support groups, case management services, information and referral to other community resources. Space is limited and there may be a waitlist. Also offers diapers when available
rows.eligibility: People experiencing homelessness, age 18 and over, and their children
rows.apply: Call to check availability and to be added to the waitlist
rows.notes: Old URL redirects to Haywood Rd Corps page (750 Haywood Rd) which is the food pantry site; transitional housing details not on this corps page.

rows.service: Offers a monthly bus pass to older adults that can be used on City of Asheville's ART bus system
rows.eligibility: Age 65 and over
rows.notes: Site redirects to buncombenc.gov/MountainMobility; office hours 8am-5pm match.

rows.service: Offers emergency financial help with electric bills only. Not able to help with deposits. Payment goes directly to the vendor. May offer household goods for people involved in natural disasters or house fires. Funding is limited. Agency is usually able to help 10 - 15 clients per month, on a first-come, first-served basis
rows.eligibility: Meet income limits and be in a crisis situation. Priority is given to homes with minors or people with health conditions
rows.apply: Call, email, or leave a voicemail to check if funding is available and schedule an appointment
rows.documents: Photo ID, proof of household income, copies of monthly bills or receipts, and a final disconnect/shut-off notice
rows.notes: Old URL redirects to Haywood Rd Corps page; specific utility assistance details not on this corps page.

rows.service: Offers a food pantry with canned and dry goods
rows.eligibility: People within the town limits
rows.notes: Site lacks phone, address, hours; could not verify US 17 N location. Kept 211 values.

rows.service: Offers services and activities for older adults. Offers exercise classes, games, and day and overnight trips. Also offers seasonal/holiday parties, arts and crafts, and more. Offers a congregate lunch meal for older adults
rows.schedule: Wellness Center hours vary by location. Call for more information. Lunch is served Monday - Friday, 11:30am
rows.eligibility: Age 60 and over
rows.apply: Call. Must register in advance for meals

rows.service: Offers a food pantry for older adults and people with disabilities
rows.schedule: Appointments are scheduled on the first, second, and fourth Friday of the month, 9:30am - 11:30am. Closed the fifth Friday of the month
rows.eligibility: Resident of Ayden or Grifton, age 55 and over, or people with disabilities

rows.service: Offers rental properties with rent based on income
rows.eligibility: Meet income limits and complete a background check
rows.apply: Call for waitlist information or visit website to apply for housing
rows.documents: Photo ID, Social Security Card, birth certificate, and proof of income

rows.service: Offers a food pantry with shelf-stable food, frozen meat, baked goods, and fresh produce. Also offers hygiene products and clothing when available
rows.schedule: 2nd and 4th Saturday of the month, 9:30am - 11:00am. Winter hours may be an hour earlier. Call to verify
rows.eligibility: Open to all
rows.notes: Site confirms 2nd and 4th Saturdays 9:30-11:00am, address, and phone. Site notes occasional closures (e.g., Bahama Day). Provides clothing in addition to food.

rows.service: Offers services and activities for older adults. Offers exercise classes, games, seasonal/holiday parties, and more. Offers a congregate lunch meal for older adults
rows.eligibility: Age 55 and over. Age 60 and over for congregate meals
rows.apply: Visit. Call to register before 10:00am for a congregate meal

rows.service: Homebound older adults receive nutritious meals five days a week. Meals are prepared daily in the Senior Center kitchen and delivered by dedicated volunteers
rows.eligibility: Homebound, age 60 and over and unable to prepare meals or attend a congregate lunch site

rows.agency: DEPARTMENT OF PUBLIC HEALTH - MITCHELL COUNTY
rows.service: A nutrition program for pregnant or postpartum women, infants, and children up to age 5. Offers nutrition education, breastfeeding support, and vouchers for healthy food and baby formula. Offers diapers. May also offer vouchers for car seats
rows.eligibility: Pregnant or postpartum women who meet income limits and have an infant or child up to age 5 at medical or nutritional risk. Men who meet income limits can also apply for their infant or child up to age 5 who is at medical or nutritional risk
rows.apply: Call or visit office to schedule an appointment. Can also complete form online at nutritionnc.com/wic/wic-referral.asp (https://www.nutritionnc.com/wic/wic-referral.asp). Someone from WIC will reach out within 20 days to schedule an appointment
rows.documents: Proof of income, proof of address, and one of the following for proof of identification for each person applying: Medicaid card, Social Security card, birth certificate, pay stub, immunization record, or hospital ID card
rows.notes: Site lists Mitchell campus main phone as 828-688-2371; 211 had 828-733-6052 (Avery number).

rows.service: Offers services and activities for older adults. Offers exercise classes, games, and day trips. Also offers seasonal/holiday parties, health screenings, health education programs, and more. Offers information and referrals to community resources. Offers a congregate lunch meal for older adults at Currituck Senior Center. Also offers classes to help people learn to use computers and search the internet
rows.schedule: Currituck Senior Center: Monday - Friday, 8:00am - 5:00pm. Knotts Island Senior Center: Monday - Friday, 9:00am - 3:30pm. Powells Point Senior Center: Monday - Friday, 8:00am - 4:30pm
rows.eligibility: Age 55 and over. Age 60 and over for congregate meals
rows.notes: Site lists 252-232-3505 as Currituck Senior Center main phone at 130 Community Way; 211 had Knotts Island number.

rows.agency: NORTH CAROLINA COOPERATIVE EXTENSION CENTER - CURRITUCK COUNTY
rows.service: Offers forestry management services and agricultural production information. Offers nutrition information workshops and food safety education. Also offers youth programs such as 4-H activities and clubs
rows.eligibility: Open to all. Youth programs available for children and adolescents, age 5 - 19. Other programs may have more specific eligibility requirements
rows.apply: Call, visit the office, or visit website to learn more about programs. Some may require registration

rows.service: Delivers meals to homebound older adults
rows.schedule: Currituck Senior Center: Monday - Friday, 8:00am - 5:00pm. Knotts Island Senior Center: Monday - Friday, 9:00am - 3:30pm. Powells Point Senior Center: Monday - Friday, 8:00am - 4:30pm. Meals are delivered Monday - Friday around 12:00pm
rows.eligibility: Homebound, age 60 and over, and unable to prepare meals or attend a congregate lunch site
rows.documents: Photo ID and proof of address

rows.service: Offers an emergency food pantry. Offers produce and deli items when available. Offers a thrift store that sells donated clothes and household items like bedding, appliances, furniture, and more. Also offers recreational facilities and special community events
rows.schedule: Food Pantry: Tuesday, 1:00pm - 6:00pm Wednesday, 11:00am - 12:00pm Thursday, 9:00am - 12:00pm. Thrift Store: Tuesday, 11:00am - 3:00pm Thursday,1:00pm - 4:00pm. Friday, 11:00am - 2:00pm. Pantry and Store hours may vary. Call to confirm hours
rows.eligibility: Residents of Barnardsville and surrounding areas of Weaverville, Mars Hill, and Jupiter
rows.apply: Visit the office, call, or visit website to learn more about programs and facilities. Some may require registration
rows.notes: Pantry Tue 1-6pm, Wed 11-2pm, Thu 9-12pm; matches 211.

rows.service: This charity is part of the Food Bank of the Albemarle. Everything from Summer meals, Commodity Supplemental Food, and free groceries are offered
rows.schedule: Hours vary, call for more information
rows.eligibility: Open to all
rows.notes: Site confirms address and phone; food pantry not separately featured on church site.

rows.agency: NORTH CAROLINA COOPERATIVE EXTENSION CENTER - PAMLICO COUNTY
rows.service: Offers forestry management services and agricultural production information. Offers nutrition information workshops and food safety education. Also offers youth programs such as 4-H activities and clubs
rows.eligibility: Open to all. Youth programs available for children and adolescents, age 5 - 19. Other programs may have more specific eligibility requirements
rows.apply: Call, visit the office, or visit website to learn more about programs. Some may require registration

rows.agency: DEPARTMENT OF SOCIAL SERVICES - PAMLICO COUNTY
rows.service: Special Assistance offers a cash supplement to help pay for room and board in an adult care home or group home. Special Assistance In-Home offers a cash supplement to help people who are at risk of entering a residential facility but prefer to live at home. Long Term Care offers a cash supplement to help pay for room and board in a long term care or skilled nursing facility. Payments are made to the vendor
rows.eligibility: Meet income limits, be eligible for an approved residential or skilled nursing facility, and have a disability or be age 65 and over. Must be a US citizen or eligible alien
rows.apply: Special Assistance Programs: Visit to complete an application or call to have it mailed. Long Term Care: Visit to complete an application, call to have it mailed, or apply online at epass.nc.gov (https://epass.nc.gov/)
rows.documents: Special Assistance: Last 3 years of bank statements, proof of household income and resources, proof of monthly expenses, and the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household. Long Term Care: Last 5 years of bank statements, an FL-2 form from a doctor, proof of household income and resources, proof of monthly expenses, and the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household
rows.notes: Pamlico County DSS subpage could not be reached; homepage references DSS but did not provide program details.

rows.agency: DEPARTMENT OF SOCIAL SERVICES - PAMLICO COUNTY
rows.service: Offers health insurance for people with low income and limited resources. Medicaid transportation is available to Medicaid recipients who need assistance getting to Medicaid-approved medical appointments and providers
rows.eligibility: Medicaid : Eligibility based on age, pregnancy status, disability status, income, and resources. Must be a US citizen or eligible alien. Medicaid Transportation: People already receiving Medicaid through Medicaid Direct or the Eastern Band of Cherokee Indians tribal health plan. People already receiving Medicaid through a different health plan (AmeriHealth Caritas, Carolina Complete Health, HealthyBlue, UnitedHealthcare, or WellCare) must contact their plan provider for transportation
rows.apply: Medicaid : Visit to complete an application, call to have one mailed, or apply online at epass.nc.gov (https://epass.nc.gov/). Medicaid Transportation: Call to schedule medical transportation
rows.documents: Proof of household income and resources, proof of monthly expenses, and the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household
rows.notes: Pamlico County DSS subpage could not be reached; homepage references DSS but did not provide program details.

rows.agency: DEPARTMENT OF SOCIAL SERVICES - PAMLICO COUNTY
rows.service: Offers temporary financial help for people in a crisis. Crisis Intervention Program (CIP): Offers financial help with utility bills for heating or cooling emergencies. Payments are made to the vendor. Help is available more than once every 12 months
rows.eligibility: Meet income limits and be at risk of a health-related emergency if utilities are shut off. May require a utility shut-off notice to be eligible. Must be a US citizen or eligible alien
rows.apply: Call to see if funds are available and to see if an application can be completed over the phone. Visit to complete an application or call to have 1 mailed
rows.documents: Proof of crisis, photo ID, birth certificate, proof of household income, copy of utility bill or payment receipt, and the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household
rows.notes: Pamlico County DSS subpage could not be reached; homepage references DSS but did not provide program details.

rows.service: Offers rental properties with rent based on income
rows.eligibility: Meet income limits and complete a background check
rows.documents: Photo ID, Social Security card, birth certificate, and proof of income
rows.notes: Site does not list specific address, phone, or hours; keeping 211 data.

rows.agency: DEPARTMENT OF SOCIAL SERVICES - CARTERET COUNTY
rows.service: Offers health insurance for people with low income and limited resources. Medicaid transportation is available to Medicaid recipients who need assistance getting to Medicaid-approved medical appointments and providers
rows.eligibility: Medicaid : Eligibility based on age, pregnancy status, disability status, income, and resources. Must be a US citizen or eligible alien. Medicaid Transportation: People already receiving Medicaid through Medicaid Direct or the Eastern Band of Cherokee Indians tribal health plan. People already receiving Medicaid through a different health plan (AmeriHealth Caritas, Carolina Complete Health, HealthyBlue, UnitedHealthcare, or WellCare) must contact their plan provider for transportation
rows.apply: Medicaid : Visit to complete an application, call to have 1 mailed, or apply online at epass.nc.gov (https://epass.nc.gov/). Medicaid Transportation: Call to schedule medical transportation
rows.documents: Proof of household income and resources, proof of monthly expenses, and the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household

rows.agency: DEPARTMENT OF SOCIAL SERVICES - CARTERET COUNTY
rows.service: Special Assistance offers a cash supplement to help pay for room and board in an adult care home or group home. Special Assistance In-Home offers a cash supplement to help people who are at risk of entering a residential facility but prefer to live at home. Long Term Care offers a cash supplement to help pay for room and board in a long term care or skilled nursing facility. Payments are made to the vendor
rows.eligibility: Meet income limits, live in an approved residential or skilled nursing facility, and have a disability or be age 65 and over. Must be a US citizen or eligible alien
rows.apply: Special Assistance: Visit to complete an application or call to have 1 mailed. Long Term Care: Visit to complete an application, call to have 1 mailed, or apply online at https://epass.nc.gov/ (https://epass.nc.gov/)
rows.documents: Special Assistance: Last 3 years of bank statements, proof of household income and resources, proof of monthly expenses, and the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household. Long Term Care: Last 5 years of bank statements, an FL-2 form from a doctor, proof of household income and resources, proof of monthly expenses, and the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household

rows.agency: DEPARTMENT OF SOCIAL SERVICES - CARTERET COUNTY
rows.service: Offers temporary financial help for people in a crisis. Crisis Intervention Program (CIP): Offers financial help with utility bills for heating or cooling emergencies. Payments are made to the vendor. Help is available more than once every 12 months
rows.eligibility: Meet income limits and be at risk of a health-related emergency if utilities are shut off. May require a utility shut-off notice to be eligible
rows.apply: Call to see if funds are available and to see if an application can be completed over the phone. Visit office to complete an application, call to have 1 mailed, or apply online
rows.documents: Proof of crisis, photo ID, birth certificate, proof of household income, copy of utility bill or payment receipt, and the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household

rows.service: Offers food, clothing, furniture, and household items. Also offers financial help for utilities, rent, medicine, and heating fuel. Limited funds available. Offers back-to-school and Christmas programs
rows.eligibility: Meet income limits. Does not cover all of Belmont. Their coverage area is the west side of Belmont to the Cramer city limit and down the Pensula. The cut off is the east side, the ramp on 85 down Parks street
rows.documents: Photo ID, Social Security number, proof of address, and proof of income

rows.agency: SERVING OUR COMMUNITY WITH KINDNESS IN SPRINGWOOD
rows.service: Offers a food pantry and clothing closet. Also offers financial assistance for electric bill payment
rows.eligibility: Residents of North Belmont or the Springwood Community
rows.apply: Visit. Will need to confirm that their address falls within the service area
rows.documents: Proof of address, proof of income, and number of people in the household
rows.notes: No website provided in batch input; unable to verify against agency site.

rows.service: Offers rental properties with rent based on income
rows.eligibility: Meet income limits and pass a background check
rows.apply: Call or visit website for waitlist information
rows.documents: Photo ID, Social Security card, birth certificate, proof of income
rows.notes: Site lists Flowers Court office hours Mon-Thu 8am-5pm (Friday closed); 211 schedule conflicts.

rows.service: Offers long-term transitional shelter for women experiencing homelessness. Case management, community referrals, financial counseling, mental health counseling, and more are available for residents
rows.eligibility: Women age 18 and over who are experiencing homelessness or at risk of homelessness. Must not be active substance user or have male children over age 10. Must be able to climb stairs and complete household chores. Must have no other safe housing options. Cannot have stayed at Catherine's House within the past 24 months. Unable to accept pregnant women and women that have more than 3 children. Must have own transportation
rows.apply: Call for initial phone screening. Does not offer same-day acceptance
rows.notes: Address 141 Mercy Dr Belmont, phone 704-825-9599, Mon-Fri 8:30am-5:30pm hours all match site.

rows.agency: DEPARTMENT OF SOCIAL SERVICES - BRUNSWICK COUNTY
rows.service: Special Assistance offers a cash supplement to help pay for room and board in an adult care home or group home. Special Assistance In-Home offers a cash supplement to help people who are at risk of entering a residential facility but prefer to live at home. Long Term Care offers a cash supplement to help pay for room and board in a long term care or skilled nursing facility. Payments are made to the vendor
rows.eligibility: Meet income limits, be eligible for an approved residential or skilled nursing facility, and have a disability or be age 65 and over. Must be a US citizen or eligible alien

rows.documents: Special Assistance Programs: Last 3 years of bank statements, proof of household income and resources, proof of monthly expenses, and the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household. Long Term Care: Last 5 years of bank statements, an FL-2 form from a doctor, proof of household income and resources, proof of monthly expenses, and the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household

Medicaid Transportation: People already receiving Medicaid through Medicaid Direct or the Eastern Band of Cherokee Indians tribal health plan. People already receiving Medicaid through a different health plan (AmeriHealth Caritas, Carolina Complete Health, HealthyBlue, UnitedHealthcare, or WellCare) must contact their plan provider for transportation
rows.apply: Medicaid : Visit to complete an application, call to have it mailed, or apply online at epass.nc.gov (https://epass.nc.gov/). Medicaid Transportation: Call to schedule medical transportation
rows.documents: Proof of household income and resources, proof of monthly expenses, and the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household

rows.service: Offers temporary financial help for people in a crisis. Crisis Intervention Program (CIP) : Offers financial help with utility bills for heating or cooling emergencies. Payments are made to the utility provider. Help is available more than once every 12 months. Other financial help may be available for rent, transportation and temporary shelter, based on circumstances. Limited funds available
rows.eligibility: Crisis Intervention Program: Meet income limits and be at risk of a health-related emergency if utilities are shut off. May require a utility shut-off notice to be eligible. Requirements for other financial help may vary

rows.agency: DEPARTMENT OF SOCIAL SERVICES - TRANSYLVANIA COUNTY
rows.service: Special Assistance offers a cash supplement to help pay for room and board in an adult care home or group home. Special Assistance In-Home offers a cash supplement to help people who are at risk of entering a residential facility but prefer to live at home. Long Term Care offers a cash supplement to help pay for room and board in a long term care or skilled nursing facility. Payments are made to the vendor
rows.eligibility: Meet income limits, be eligible for an approved residential or skilled nursing facility, and have a disability or be age 65 and over
rows.apply: Special Assistance Programs: Visit to complete an application or call to have it mailed. Long Term Care: Visit to complete an application, call to have it mailed, or apply online at epass.nc.gov (https://epass.nc.gov/)
rows.documents: Special Assistance Programs: Last 3 years of bank statements, proof of household income and resources, proof of monthly expenses, and the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household. Long Term Care: Last 5 years of bank statements, an FL-2 form from a doctor, proof of household income and resources, proof of monthly expenses, and the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household
rows.notes: Site confirms Special Assistance and Long Term Care at 106 East Morgan St Brevard, phone 828-884-3174.

rows.apply: Call to see if funds are available. Visit to complete an application, call to have one mailed, or apply online at epass.nc.gov (https://epass.nc.gov/). NOTE: Households with a person age 60 and over or with a person under age 60 who is receiving services from the NC Division of Aging and Adult Services can apply beginning December 1. All other households can apply beginning January 1

rows.notes: Site confirms LIEAP/utility assistance at 106 East Morgan St Brevard, phone 828-884-3174.

Must be one of the following: - Active-duty Sailors, Marines, and their dependents or surviving spouses, or - Medically retired, or retired after 20+ years of service, Sailors, Marines, and their dependents or surviving spouses, or - Reservists on extended active duty of 30 days or more. Veterans that did not retire do not qualify
rows.apply: Call to schedule an appointment. Print and complete budget to bring to the appointment
rows.documents: Completed budget, military ID, recent LES or Retired Annuity Statement, and bills or other documents about financial need

Veterans that did not retire do not qualify

rows.service: Offers financial help for medical needs or quality of life enhancements. Needs includes therapy not covered by insurance, handicap accessible home and care modifications, mobiity items, and more. Cannot help with past due medical bills, funeral costs, and experimental medical procedures
rows.schedule: Website is available 24 hours a day
rows.eligibility: People with rare medical conditions, honorably discharged veterans with a medically-related needs. and first responders with medically-related need. May only apply once for assistance. 501(c)3 organizations that help veterans, first responders, or people with rare medical conditions can apply for a grant. Nonprofits may be disqualified if their annual revenue is below $200,000 or above $10.000.000
rows.documents: Signed medical diagnosis and prognosis from a healthcare professional, medical care team contact information, proof of income, and list of items requested. First responders must provide proof of current or past employment as a first responder. Veterans must provide DD-214 and military ID or VA Benefits card. Nonprofits must provide their 990 form from the past 2 years and federal determination letter

rows.service: Offers escorted door-to-door transportation for medical appointments, grocery shopping, pharmacy visits, and banking
rows.eligibility: Age 65 and over, or age 18 and over with a disability. Medical appointment transportation not offered to Medicaid recipients. Volunteer drivers cannot transport a wheelchair
rows.documents: Photo ID showing birth date for adults age 65 and over, or documentation of disability from a medical doctor for adults with disabilities

rows.service: Offers support for individuals with disabilities who are experiencing homelessness. Offers safe and stable housing, covering rent cost, and providing ongoing support to help individuals remain housed long-term
rows.eligibility: Individuals with disabilities
rows.apply: Call or visit website for more information
rows.notes: Agency site lists Member Service Line Mon-Sat 7am-6pm (1-800-962-9003) and 24/7 crisis line 1-800-849-6127.

rows.service: Offers financial help for rent and utilities to food service workers. Financial help is given based on financial need and type of crisis. Does not pay for medical expenses
rows.eligibility: Food service workers in restaurants, catering, food trucks, concessions, cafeterias, coffee shops, and bars/taprooms who experienced a qualifying crisis (injury, illness, death of a family member, or housing disaster) within the past four months
rows.apply: Apply online at givingkitchen.org/help. A caseworker contacts the applicant within 2-3 days; supporting documents requested if eligible
rows.documents: None to apply. Documents that may be required after application are poof of employment in food service, proof of income, lease agreement, and current utility bill. Other documents are required based on crisis such as a doctor's note with expected time of missed work or accident report. Employment and crisis is verified with the employer. Employee must be able to show financial need. Visit website for more information on specific documents that may be required
rows.notes: thegivingkitchen.org redirects to givingkitchen.org; site adds 'within past 4 months' eligibility window and notes 2-3 day caseworker contact (verified 2026-05-01); HQ in Atlanta GA, no Cary NC office

rows.service: Offers financial help for people in a crisis. Helps with rent or mortgage, utilities, home repairs, and emergency lodging. Helps with medical bills, mental health bills, dental bills, and prescription medications. Also helps with transportation, car repairs, and car insurance. Limited funds available
rows.eligibility: Meet income limits and be in a crisis situation. Live in Cary or Morrisville or have a child enrolled in a Cary or Morrisville school. Individuals can only receive help once a year
rows.apply: Apply online. Watch out for a call or email for follow up
rows.documents: Photo ID, proof of residence in Cary or Morrisville, proof of income or unemployment, lease, past-due bills, and proof of the crisis situation
rows.notes: Per neighborup.org 2026-05-01, Cary campus address is 187 High House Rd (not 189). Hours, phone, eligibility match 211.

rows.service: Offers a 4-month housing program and 12-month graduate program for working families with children experiencing homelessness. Residents receive life skills training such as budgeting, career planning, and more
rows.eligibility: Working families with school age and younger children who are experiencing homelessness and meet income limits. Adults must be working full-time and should be the guardian/parent of at least 1 child
rows.documents: Social Security card for everyone in the household, proof of income, and photo ID. Background check and credit check will be needed to determine services and resources needed

rows.service: Offers transitional housing for people impacted by a natural disaster
rows.schedule: Hours vary, visit website for more information
rows.eligibility: Veterans, first responders, and families displaced by natural disaster
rows.notes: Per emergencyrv.org 2026-05-01, eligibility is limited to veterans, first responders, and families displaced by natural disaster (not 'open to all' as 211 states). Apply via Survivor Intake form online.

rows.service: Offers emergency shelter, transitional housing, legal assistance, and support services for victims of domestic violence and their children

rows.service: Offers referrals to homeless shelters in the area. Same-day shelter is not available, but people can be added to the waitlist for when there is a vacancy. May also offer resources for housing repair, help with eviction, and more. People MUST go through Coordinated Entry to get access to a shelter. Housing Helpline staff cannot assist with housing searches
rows.schedule: Housing Helpline: Monday - Friday, 10:00am - 4:00pm May also leave a voicemail or email at any time. In-Person Drop-In Hours: At Hillsborough Commons Wednesday, 8:30am - 4:00pm. At Inter-Faith Council Monday, 11:00am - 2:00pm. At Southern Human Services Center Monday - Friday, 8:30am - 4:00pm
