rows.service: Local volunteers work with eligible families to build homes. Qualified homebuyers pay back a mortgage over 30 years
rows.eligibility: Meet income limits, have lived in the area for the last 12 months, and are willing to contribute "sweat equity" volunteer hours. Some people may be eligible if they have only worked in the area for the last 12 months, rather than lived in the area. Must have ability to pay a monthly mortgage, and pass a credit and criminal background check
rows.apply: Fill out the Homebuyer Interest form online
rows.documents: Proof of household income and address
rows.notes: Address (88 Vilcom Center Drive, Suite L110), phone, and homeownership application confirmed; site does not post specific hours.

rows.agency: DEPARTMENT OF SOCIAL SERVICES - ORANGE COUNTY
rows.service: Special Assistance offers a cash supplement to help pay for room and board in an adult care home or group home. Special Assistance In-Home offers a cash supplement to help people who are at risk of entering a residential facility but prefer to live at home. Long Term Care offers a cash supplement to help pay for room and board in a long term care or skilled nursing facility. Payments are made to the vendor
rows.eligibility: Meet income limits, live in an approved residential or skilled nursing facility, and have a disability or be age 65 and over
rows.apply: Special Assistance Programs: Visit to complete an application or call to have it mailed. Long Term Care: Visit to complete an application, call to have it mailed, or apply online at epass.nc.gov (https://epass.nc.gov/)
rows.documents: Special Assistance Programs: Last 3 years of bank statements, proof of household income and resources, proof of monthly expenses, and the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household. Long Term Care: Last 5 years of bank statements, an FL-2 form from a doctor, proof of household income and resources, proof of monthly expenses, and the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household
rows.notes: Per agency site Chapel Hill DSS now at Southern Human Services, 2501 Homestead Rd, 27516 (211 had 828 MLK Jr Blvd, 27514).

rows.agency: DEPARTMENT OF SOCIAL SERVICES - ORANGE COUNTY
rows.service: Offers temporary financial help for people in a crisis. Crisis Intervention Program (CIP): Offers financial help with utility bills for heating or cooling emergencies. Payments are made to the utility provider. Help is available more than once every 12 months
rows.eligibility: Meet income limits and be at risk of a health-related emergency if utilities are shut off. May require a utility shut-off notice to be eligible. Must be a US citizen or eligible alien
rows.apply: Call to see if funds are available and to see if an application can be completed over the phone. Visit to complete an application or call to have 1 mailed
rows.documents: Proof of crisis, photo ID, birth certificate, proof of household income, copy of utility bill or payment receipt, and the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household
rows.notes: Per agency site Chapel Hill DSS now at Southern Human Services, 2501 Homestead Rd, 27516 (211 had 828 MLK Jr Blvd, 27514).

rows.service: Offers home repairs. Offers painting/staining, roof repair, heating system repair, and more. Also offers modifications for accessibility, such as wheelchair ramps or bathroom modifications
rows.schedule: Mon 8:00am - 5:00pm; Tue 8:00am - 5:00pm; Wed 8:00am - 5:00pm; Thu 8:00am - 5:00pm; Fri 8:00am - 5:00pm; Sat 8:00am - 12:00pm;
rows.eligibility: Meet income limits and have owned and lived in the home for at least 5 years. For a mobile home, must also own the land under the mobile home. Veterans encouraged to apply. The home must also be located in approved zip codes within Habitat Charlotte Region's service area
rows.apply: Visit the website to check if your zip code is approved and apply online at https://habitatcltregion.tfaforms.net/f/inquiryform (https://habitatcltregion.tfaforms.net/f/inquiryform). If you are not eligible at this time, you can fill out an interest form to be notified when applications are being accepted
rows.documents: Proof of income and homeownership. Also provide proof of land ownership for mobile home
rows.notes: habitatcharlotte.org redirects to habitatcltregion.org. Site shows Saturday 8am-12pm hours.

rows.service: Offers home repairs, safety upgrades, and health-focused improvements in targeted neighborhoods
rows.eligibility: Residents of Davidson West, Smithville, East Catawba, Pottstown, Eastwood Acres, and Hampshire Hills
rows.apply: Call or visit website for more information
rows.documents: Proof of income, assets, homeowners insurance
rows.notes: Site shows updated address and earlier opening time.

rows.service: Offers home repairs and help with yard work. Offers exterior painting and minor construction such as porches and steps. Also offers modifications for accessibility, such as wheelchair ramps. Not able to help with plumbing, masonry, electrical work, floor and roof repair, underpining, or other major home renovations. Repairs are made during work group retreats
rows.eligibility: Open to all. Services are only offered during the summer. Referrals are accepted year-round but only complete projects over the summer
rows.apply: Visit website to fill out an online application
rows.documents: Proof of homeownership or proof of homeowner's permission if the family is renting
rows.notes: Site lists 3540 Toringdon Way Ste 200 #208 Charlotte 28277 phone 704-284-9704; prior 211 had blank address and phone 704-237-0349.

rows.service: Offers minor home repairs and modifications for older adults whose homes are a risk to their health and safety
rows.eligibility: Age 55 and over with a home that is a threat to health and safety. Must be homeowners. Provide small electrical repairs, minor carpentry work, light painting and other small projects

rows.service: Offer low-interest loans and grants to older homeowners and homeowners with disabilities to make accessibility and other modifications. Housing Rehabilitation: Makes major repairs, such as roof problems, heating systems, wells/septic systems, and more. Lead Hazard: Makes repairs to lead-based items in the home to remove them. Aging in Place: Makes repairs for older adults so their homes are safer. Repairs may include wheelchair ramps, grab bars, and more. Home Repair: Makes minor repairs, such as painting, floor issues, minor electrical problems, and more
rows.eligibility: Homeowners who live in their home and who meet income limits. The Aging in Place program also requires homeowners to be older adults
rows.documents: Proof of income and home ownership
rows.notes: Charlotte gov site returns 403 forbidden to WebFetch; could not verify, kept 211 data.

rows.service: Offers major home repairs for homeowners. Repairs may include leaking roofs, lack of heat, plumbing, and electrical problems. Also offers accessibility modifications for people with disabilities
rows.eligibility: Meet income limits, and intend to live in home for at least 5 years after repairs. Must have or be willing to obtain homeowners insurance. Must be current on taxes and not have any liens
rows.apply: Call or visit website for more information
rows.documents: Proof of income, assets, homeowners insurance, and paid property taxes. Full list available during application process
rows.notes: Site shows updated address and earlier opening time.

rows.service: Offers one-on-one homebuyer counseling and self-guided homebuyer education

rows.notes: Site confirms phone 866-889-9347 and HUD-certified housing counseling; specific Charlotte office hours not listed online.

rows.apply: Requires a referral through a partner organization. A list of organizations can be found at https://furnishforgood.org/existing-partners/ (https://furnishforgood.org/existing-partners/)

rows.agency: DEPARTMENT OF SOCIAL SERVICES - MECKLENBURG COUNTY
rows.service: Health insurance for people with low income and limited resources. Medicaid Transportation is available to Medicaid participants who need assistance getting to Medicaid-approved appointments and providers
rows.eligibility: Medicaid : Eligibility based on age, pregnancy status, disability status, income, and resources. Medicaid Transportation: People already receiving Medicaid through Medicaid Direct or the Eastern Band of Cherokee Indians tribal health plan. People already receiving Medicaid through a different health plan (AmeriHealth Caritas, Carolina Complete Health, HealthyBlue, UnitedHealthcare, or WellCare) must contact their plan provider for transportation
rows.apply: Medicaid : Visit to complete an application, call to have one mailed, or apply online at epass.nc.gov (https://epass.nc.gov/). Medicaid Transportation: Call to schedule medical transportation
rows.documents: Proof of household income and resources, proof of monthly expenses, and the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household
rows.notes: mecknc.gov/dss returned 403 Forbidden; could not verify.

rows.service: Offers transportation to and from treatment for people with cancer
rows.eligibility: Cancer patients traveling for a cancer-related medical appointment. Must be able to walk unassisted to and from the vehicle, or have an accompanying caregiver to assist. Patients under the age of 18 must be accompanied by a legally responsible adult such as a parent or caregiver
rows.apply: Call at least 3 business days in advance, not including the date transportation is needed

rows.service: Offers transportation to medical appointments, pharmacies, and grocery stores for older adults
rows.eligibility: Age 55 and over. Must be able to transfer in and out of the vehicle independently
rows.apply: Call or visit the office. Rides must be scheduled 5 business days in advance

rows.service: Offers bankruptcy counseling, credit counseling, and a debt management program. Also offers foreclosure prevention counseling, homebuyer counseling, and payday loan counseling
rows.eligibility: Open to all
rows.apply: Call, email or visit website for more information

rows.service: Offers help to people with low income to learn how to manage money and become more financially stable. The program offers classes, one-on-one help, access to banking, and no-interest loans. The goal is to help individuals become more independent and rely less on emergency assistance
rows.eligibility: Open to all

rows.service: Offers homeownership counseling and education, financial literacy, and foreclosure prevention. Also offers homebuyer down payment assistance loans. For rental listing visit: dreamkeypartners.org/rental-properties (https://dreamkeypartners.org/rental-properties/)
rows.eligibility: Meet income limits. Homebuyer down payment assistance loans are for residents in the City of Charlotte only

rows.service: Offers clean injecting supplies and a place to dispose of used needles and syringes. Offers testing for sexually transmitted infections, HIV, and Hepatitis C. Offers safe sex supplies, including condoms. Offers naloxone and overdose reversal and response training. Also offers outdoor gear such as tents and sleeping bags, hygiene kits, food, water, and more
rows.eligibility: Open to all
rows.apply: Call, text, or visit the office

rows.service: Offers a program of monthly workshops for women to learn independent living skills, financial literacy, job readiness, and more. Also offers mentoring and enrichment opportunities. Offer bags with personal toiletries and hygiene care items

rows.service: Offers overnight shelter when temperatures drop below 32 degrees. Space is limited and there may only be a cot available instead of a shelter bed

rows.eligibility: Women and children (boys age 12 or younger, girls of any age) experiencing homelessness

rows.documents: None. Proof of employment may be needed to access the shelter at later times, although they may be full by then
rows.notes: Center of Hope at 534 Spratt St verified; site does not provide intake hours or eligibility specifics, kept 211 schedule.

rows.service: Offers a safe place to stay for youth who have run away from home, are experiencing homelessness, or are going through a crisis. Also offers drop-in crisis support and after-care services
rows.eligibility: Youth age 7 - 17

rows.service: Offers fixed-route bus, light rail, and streetcar services, with schedules that vary by route. Also offers car and van pool services, and bike rental services. Find real-time route information by downloading the CATS-Pass app
rows.schedule: Call Center Hours: Monday - Friday, 5:30am - 7:00pm Saturday and Sunday, 7:00am - 11:00am and 12:30pn - 4:00pm Service Hours: Vary, depending on service and route. Call or visit website for more information
rows.eligibility: Open to all
rows.apply: Call, download app, or visit website to find route information

rows.service: Offers services to veterans who are low-income and/or experiencing homelessness and their families. Offers outreach, case management, and help getting VA and other public benefits. Also offers short-term financial help with rent
rows.eligibility: Veterans who are experiencing homelessness and/or meet income limits. Unable to help veterans who have a dishonorable discharge
rows.documents: Photo ID, Social Security card, children's birth certificates, marriage licenses, DD214, VA medical card
rows.notes: Charlotte regional office main line per site is 704-370-3262; 211 had toll-free 855-962-8387; SSVF program for veterans confirmed.

rows.service: Offers financial help with past-due rent and utilities. Payment goes directly to the vendor. Limited funding available. Applications are prioritized by most urgent need
rows.eligibility: Rent Assistance : People with overdue rent. Must only be 1 month past due. Must reside in a home for 60 days before asking for assistance. Utility Assistance : People with overdue utility bills or disconnection notices for electricity, gas, or water
rows.apply: Applications are accepted at office beginning at 10:00am. People who are homebound with an assigned healthcare worker may ask for a partner referral. Applications can also be picked up and completed offsite to be returned along with required documents beginning at 10:00am. There may be a wait to submit documents. Once documents are reviewed, casework and eligibility interviews occur by phone. Details on required documents can be found on the website (https://crisisassistance.org/programs/basic-needs/emergency-financial-assistance/#whattobring)
rows.documents: Photo ID, proof of address, Social Security cards for everyone in the household (if registered), proof of household income for the last 30 days including earned income, self-employment, disability or other benefit payments. For Utility Assistance : Past due utility bill or disconnection notice. For Rental Assistance : Copy of lease and past due rent or eviction notice
rows.notes: Site lists address as 500-A Spratt St.

rows.service: Offers financial help with rental deposits and rent for people living with HIV/AIDS
rows.eligibility: Must be medically diagnosed with HIV/AIDS and be a US citizen or have immigration status
rows.documents: Photo ID, proof of diagnosis, proof of monthly expenses and income, and proof of address
rows.notes: raoassist.org could not be verified due to network restrictions; unable to verify program details.

rows.agency: COMMUNITY SUPPORT SERVICES OF MECKLENBURG COUNTY
rows.service: Offers assessment, screening, and referrals to homeless shelters in the area. People MUST go through Coordinated Entry to get access to a shelter. NOTE: CE does not guarantee shelter availability or placement. CE does not provide motel vouchers, housing vouchers, transportation, housing navigation, or discharge planning
rows.eligibility: Mecklenburg County residents who are experiencing homelessness or who are at imminent risk of homelessness or an adult or people in an institution/facility being imminently discharged to homelessness in Mecklenburg County
rows.apply: Call the Coordinated Entry hotline. People can leave a voicemail for a return call. Every effort is made to return calls the same business day, or the very next. NOTE: People with mobility issues who are unable to use a top bunk need to be specific when calling - this will make it easier for staff to find an appropriate bed. There is very limited in-person intake for Coordinated Entry - this is available ONLY for people without a phone. People can visit Roof Above Day Services Center Monday - Thursday, 8:30am - 4:00pm, Valerie C. Woodard Community Center Monday - Sunday, 8:00am - 5:00pm
rows.notes: Domain could not be verified for safe fetch; unable to confirm site details, kept 211 values.

rows.service: Offers support to people experiencing homelessness. A mobile program that offers daily meals, tents, tarps, sleeping bags, clothing, toiletries, household goods, and more throughout uptown Charlotte. Also offers information and referrals, and substance use counseling. Offers Dinners nightly beginning at 5:30 except on Saturdays. Offers Free Showers and Laundry every 3rd Saturdays at 8am. Offers Free Grocery Giveaway every 2nd and 4th Saturdays from 12pm - 2pm. Does not directly offer housing
rows.schedule: Mon 10:00am - 7:00pm; Tue 10:00am - 7:00pm; Wed 10:00am - 7:00pm; Thu 10:00am - 7:00pm; Fri 10:00am - 7:00pm; Sat; Sun 10:00am - 7:00pm;

rows.service: An outreach event offering a wide variety of services to people experiencing homelessness in Charlotte. rows.schedule: Regular Shelter: Available 5:00pm - 7:00am. Curfew is at 7:00pm. People with night jobs must be in no later than 9:00pm. Soup Kitchen: Monday - Wednesday, 11:30am - 12:30pm. Cold Weather Shelter: Intake begins at 8:00pm or 8:30pm when temperature is below 32 degrees
rows.eligibility: Regular Shelter: People experiencing homelessness who can pass a breathalyzer, help with chores, and who are negative for COVID. Unable to accept sex offenders or people under the influence of drugs or alcohol. Unable to assist folks with wheelchairs and mobility limitations. Walk-in requests for housing not typically available. Cold Weather Shelter: People experiencing homelessness who are not under the influence of drugs or alcohol

rows.documents: Regular Shelter: Photo ID and negative COVID test. Cold Weather Shelter

rows.agency: DEPARTMENT OF SOCIAL SERVICES - CURRITUCK COUNTY
rows.service: Special Assistance offers a cash supplement to help pay for room and board in an adult care home or group home. Special Assistance In-Home offers a cash supplement to help people who are at risk of entering a residential facility but prefer to live at home. Long Term Care offers a cash supplement to help pay for room and board in a long term care or skilled nursing facility. Payments are made to the vendor
rows.eligibility: Meet income limits, be eligible for an approved residential or skilled nursing facility, and have a disability or be age 65 and over. Must be a US citizen or eligible alien
rows.apply: Special Assistance Programs: Visit to complete an application or call to have it mailed. Long Term Care: Visit to complete an application, call to have it mailed, or apply online at epass.nc.gov (https://epass.nc.gov/)
rows.documents: Special Assistance Programs: Last 3 years of bank statements, proof of household income and resources, proof of monthly expenses, and the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household. Long Term Care: Last 5 years of bank statements, an FL-2 form from a doctor, proof of household income and resources, proof of monthly expenses, and the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household

rows.agency: DEPARTMENT OF SOCIAL SERVICES - CURRITUCK COUNTY
rows.service: Offers health insurance for people with low income and limited resources. Medicaid transportation is available to Medicaid recipients who need assistance getting to Medicaid-approved medical appointments and providers
rows.eligibility: Medicaid: Eligibility based on age, pregnancy status, disability status, income, and resources. Must be a US citizen or eligible alien. Medicaid Transportation: People already receiving Medicaid through Medicaid Direct or the Eastern Band of Cherokee Indians tribal health plan. People already receiving Medicaid through a different health plan (AmeriHealth Caritas, Carolina Complete Health, HealthyBlue, UnitedHealthcare, or WellCare) must contact their plan provider for transportation
rows.apply: Medicaid : Visit to complete an application, call to have 1 mailed, or apply online at epass.nc.gov (https://epass.nc.gov/). Medicaid Transportation: Call to schedule medical transportation
rows.documents: Proof of household income and resources, proof of monthly expenses, and the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household

rows.agency: DEPARTMENT OF SOCIAL SERVICES - CURRITUCK COUNTY
rows.service: Offers financial help with heating bills once every 12 months. Payments are based on household size, income, and heating source. Payments are made to the utility provider. People do not need to be in a crisis to get help
rows.eligibility: Meet income limits and resource guidelines. Each household is only eligible once, even if multiple members apply. Must be a US citizen or eligible alien
rows.apply: Call to see if funds are available. Visit to complete an application, call to have 1 mailed, or apply online at epass.nc.gov (https://epass.nc.gov/). NOTE: Households with a person age 60 and over or with a person under age 60 who is receiving services from the NC Division of Aging and Adult Services can apply beginning December 1. All other households can apply beginning January 1
rows.documents: Photo ID, birth certificate, proof of household income, copy of utility bill, latest bank statement, the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household, and proof of disability if under age 60

rows.eligibility: Resident of Durham, Orange, Chatham or Alamance Counties
rows.apply: Call to schedule an appointment or to register
rows.notes: Phone digits conflict between 211 (9193544936) and site (9193544959) - recommend phone confirmation. Site has no food-pantry page; J-Cafe is separate from pantry

rows.agency: MINISTERIO GUERREROS DE JESUCRISTO INTERNACIONAL FOOD PANTRY
rows.service: Offers a food pantry
rows.eligibility: Open to all
rows.apply: Arrive by 9:00am and add name to list
rows.notes: Agency website does not mention this program — info from 211 only; Site mentions a Food Bank ministry but provides no schedule, address, or eligibility; using 211 data only. Site lists alternate phone 919-884-9478 in addition to 919-395-7539.

rows.agency: MOUNT LEVEL MISSIONARY BAPTIST CHURCH FOOD PANTRY
rows.service: Offers a food pantry with canned, frozen, and dry goods
rows.schedule: Regular Food Pantry: January-September 2nd and 4th Thursdays, 4:00pm-5:30 pm Oct 2nd Saturday 10am-2pm. November December 1st and 2nd Thursdays, 4:00-5:30. November: 2nd and 3rd Thursday of the month, 4:00pm - 7:00pm. TEFAP: December - October: 2nd Thursday of the month, 5:30pm - 7:00pm. November: 1st Thursday of the month, 5:30pm - 7:00pm
rows.eligibility: Open to all
rows.notes: Agency website does not mention food pantry; using 211 data. Phone digits conflict between 211 (9196826466) and church main line (9194773893) - 211 number may be direct pantry line; recommend phone confirmation before referral

rows.service: Offers a food pantry
rows.schedule: Monday afternoons and evenings by appointment only. 2nd Thursday of the month, 4:00pm - 5:30pm from March to November
rows.eligibility: Open to all
rows.apply: Call to schedule an appointment for Monday food pick up. Drive thru for Thursday pick up from March to November . Cars line up in the large parking lot across the street from the hub, next to the baseball field. A calendar is available at soduco.org/events (https://soduco.org/events) to confirm Food Distribution dates
rows.notes: Site confirms Monday-by-appointment and seasonal Thursday open distributions March-November, address 5123 Revere Rd, phone 984-484-8475 — all match 211. Specific Thursday times not posted on homepage; events calendar at /events is the live schedule source.

rows.agency: RUSSELL MEMORIAL CME CHURCH FOOD PANTRY
rows.service: Offers a food pantry
rows.eligibility: Open to all

rows.agency: RUSSELL MEMORIAL CME CHURCH FOOD PANTRY
rows.service: Offers a food pantry
rows.eligibility: Open to all
rows.notes: Agency website appears dead/404 — info from 211 only; Listed nc211 URL is a third-party food-banks.org directory page that returns 403 Forbidden; church has no own website. Recommend calling 919-682-2523 to confirm Wed 9am-12pm before referral.

rows.agency: SALVATION ARMY - DURHAM, ORANGE, AND PERSON COUNTIES
rows.service: Offers emergency food. Also offers clothing when available
rows.eligibility: Open to all. Available once every 90 days
rows.apply: Call and leave a message with name and phone number. Seen by appointment only
rows.documents: Photo ID for the person applying and Social Security cards or birth certificates for everyone in the household
rows.notes: Address 909 Liberty St confirmed. Site mentions 'Free Food Friday' as one food program - this differs from the 'by appointment' model in 211 data and may be a separate distribution; worth calling to confirm which program 211 is describing.

rows.service: Collects leftover crops after harvest that would otherwise be left to rot. Food is donated to community agencies
rows.eligibility: Nonprofit 501(c)3 agencies or churches
rows.notes: Carolina regional office in Durham confirmed; phone 919-309-5402 and director Michael Binger match. Gleaning program (collecting leftover crops for nonprofit distribution) confirmed. Specific hours not posted on website. Note: this is a gleaning organization that supplies agencies, not individuals.

rows.service: Offers food, clothing, and diapers. Hygiene supplies may also be available
rows.eligibility: Clothing: Open to all. Food: Families with children age 18 and under in the home, people age 62 and over, people with disabilities, or under-employed people working less than 30 hours per week. Diapers: Parent with a child in diapers
rows.apply: Visit. Available on a first-come, first-served basis to the first 20 households for each time slot. It's recommended to arrive 10 - 15 minutes before doors open to get in line
rows.documents: Clothing: Photo ID and proof of address. Food: Photo ID, proof of address, and 1 of the following: proof of child/young adult in home, proof of age, or pay stubs/work schedule showing less than 30 hours. Diapers: Photo ID, identification for child, and proof of address
rows.notes: Agency website does not mention this program — info from 211 only; Site confirms Food Pantry & Clothing Closet at 410 Liberty St., phone 919-682-0538 (resource ext. 5287); does not publish specific food/clothing hours, so 211 schedule is the operational source.

rows.service: Offers home repairs. Repairs must address a health, safety, or accessibility need. Offers modifications for accessibility, such as wheelchair ramps or bathroom modifications
rows.eligibility: Meet income limits and have owned and lived in the home for at least 1 year
rows.apply: Visit website to download application or call or email to have an application mailed. Mail or email completed application with required documents
rows.documents: Proof of income, copy of mortgage statement, and proof of homeownership. 2 recent bank statements and copy of photo IDs for members of the household age 18 and over
rows.notes: ZIP per agency website (verified 2026-05-01): 27703, not 27707. Eligibility note: must be owner-occupant in Durham County; 12-15 month wait, not for emergencies

rows.service: Sells donated household items like small appliances, furniture, books, toys, and more. Visit website to find a retail store or donation center: www.gienc.org/ourstores (https://www.gienc.org/ourstores)
rows.schedule: Mon 9:00am - 8:30pm; Tue 9:00am - 8:30pm; Wed 9:00am - 8:30pm; Thu 9:00am - 8:30pm; Fri 9:00am - 8:30pm; Sat 9:00am - 8:30pm; Sun 12:00pm - 6:00pm;
rows.eligibility: Open to all
rows.notes: Goodwill retail/donation locations confirmed via site's store-locator. Specific 1701 TW Alexander Drive Durham hours not displayed on homepage; visit gienc.org/ourstores for specific store hours which may vary by location.

rows.service: Sells donated furniture, home goods, building materials, and appliances
rows.eligibility: Open to all
rows.notes: ReStore details redirected to trianglerestores.org; homepage doesn't detail the 5501 Durham-Chapel Hill Blvd location hours. 211 hours plausible but not directly verified on agency site.

rows.service: Offers rental properties with rent based on income
rows.eligibility: Meet income limits and complete a background check
rows.documents: Photo ID, Social Security Card, birth certificate, and proof of income
rows.notes: Office address per agency website (verified 2026-05-01): 423 Morris Street, not 330 East Main. Phone and Mon-Fri 8:30am-5:00pm hours match

rows.service: Offers financial help with a housing down payment and closing costs through a forgivable loan. Eligible homebuyers may receive up to $80,000
rows.eligibility: First-time homebuyers who meet income limits and asset limits and are buying a house within the city limits of Durham. Must have worked or resided in Durham for at least 12 months. Applicant must complete approved homebuyer education classes. Must not have owned a home within the last 3 years and plan to use the home as their primary residence. More specific eligibility requirements based on type of loan and lender
rows.apply: Call or email for more information. Visit website for a list of approved lenders
rows.documents: Completed application, photo ID, proof of household income and assets, proof of homebuyer counseling, and proof of residency or work history in Durham for the last 12 months. More documents may be needed, depending on situation
rows.notes: Agency website does not mention this program — info from 211 only; City of Durham homepage loads but does not surface the Homebuyer Assistance/Down Payment program; multiple sub-page URL attempts returned 404. Using 211 data only; recommend caller call 919-560-4570 to confirm hours and current $80,000 max.

rows.service: Offers financial help with down payments and closing costs for first-time homebuyers through a grant. Applicants who remain in the property for 3 years will have the grant forgiven. If applicants leave within 3 years, they will be required to pay back all assistance funds. Program is only available in counties that were most impacted by Hurricanes Matthew and Florence
rows.eligibility: First-time homebuyers or people who have not owned a home in the past 3 years. Must meet income and asset limits and have pre-approval from a mortgage lender. Only fixed rate mortgages are allowed
rows.apply: Visit nchousing.org/hap (https://nchousing.org/hap/) to complete online form. Someone from the agency will reach back out. Any questions should be sent via email and NOT via phone
rows.documents: Proof of county residency, income, and assets
rows.notes: CRITICAL: Homeownership Assistance Program (HAP) was discontinued/transferred to ReBuild NC (rebuild.nc.gov) - 211 still lists this URL but program moved; do not refer here. Phone 833-275-7262 may no longer be correct

rows.agency: DEPARTMENT OF SOCIAL SERVICES - DURHAM COUNTY
rows.service: Special Assistance offers a cash supplement to help pay for room and board in an adult care home or group home. Special Assistance In-Home offers a cash supplement to help people who are at risk of entering a residential facility but prefer to live at home. Long Term Care offers a cash supplement to help pay for room and board in a long term care or skilled nursing facility. Payments are made to the vendor
rows.eligibility: Meet income limits, live in an approved residential or skilled nursing facility, and have a disability or be age 65 and over
rows.apply: Special Assistance Programs: Visit to complete an application or call to have it mailed. Long Term Care: Visit to complete an application, call to have it mailed, or apply online at epass.nc.gov (https://epass.nc.gov/)
rows.documents: Special Assistance Programs: Last 3 years of bank statements, proof of household income and resources, proof of monthly expenses, and the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household. Long Term Care: Last 5 years of bank statements, an FL-2 form from a doctor, proof of household income and resources, proof of monthly expenses, and the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household
rows.notes: DSS hours per agency website (verified 2026-05-01): close 5:30pm, not 5:00pm. Special Assistance In-Home listed under Aging & Adult Services; epass.nc.gov also confirmed

rows.service: Offers low-cost rental housing. Leases 10 units
rows.eligibility: Based on household income and employment, rental history, and credit
rows.apply: Call for vacancy information and application
rows.notes: Phone and address per agency website (verified 2026-05-01); 211 phone 919-907-8573 may be outdated. Programs include Affordable Housing, Supportive Housing, Street Outreach

rows.service: Delivers meals to homebound older adults and people with disabilities. Also offers frozen meals for people not at home when meals are delivered
rows.eligibility: Homebound, age 60 and over, or under age 60 with a disability, and unable to prepare meals
rows.apply: Apply online or visit website to download application to mail, return in person, or fax when completed
rows.notes: Agency website describes broader eligibility ('older adults, convalescing, others unable to provide nutrition') without firm age-60 cutoff; using 211's specific age 60+/under-60-with-disability criteria - recommend phone confirmation for borderline cases

rows.agency: DEPARTMENT OF SOCIAL SERVICES - DURHAM COUNTY
rows.service: Offers health insurance for people with low income and limited resources. Medicaid transportation is available to Medicaid recipients who need assistance getting to Medicaid-approved medical appointments and providers
rows.eligibility: Medicaid : Eligibility based on age, pregnancy status, disability status, income, and resources. Medicaid Transportation: People already receiving Medicaid through Medicaid Direct or the Eastern Band of Cherokee Indians tribal health plan. People already receiving Medicaid through a different health plan (AmeriHealth Caritas, Carolina Complete Health, HealthyBlue, UnitedHealthcare, or WellCare) must contact their plan provider for transportation
rows.apply: Medicaid : Visit to complete an application, call to have 1 mailed, or apply online at epass.nc.gov (https://epass.nc.gov/). Medicaid Transportation: Call to schedule medical transportation
rows.documents: Proof of household income and resources, proof of monthly expenses, and the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household
rows.notes: DSS hours per agency website (verified 2026-05-01): close 5:30pm, not 5:00pm. Address 414 East Main and main phone 919-560-8000 match

rows.service: Offers curb-to-curb bus service. Door-to-door service available by request
rows.schedule: GoDurham ACCESS Office: Monday - Friday, 8:00am - 5:00pm. Durham Station: Monday - Saturday, 6:00am - 12:00am Sunday, 7:00am - 9:00pm
rows.eligibility: People age 60 and over or with disabilities who are unable to use a fixed-route bus. Stop must be located within 3/4 of a mile of a fixed-route bus stop. Also available to people who live in rural areas of Durham County or need work-related, medical, human services, government, or personal care trips
rows.apply: Visit website to download application or call to have 1 mailed. If applying based on disability, part of application requires an assessment from a licensed medical professional. Return application in person or by mail. If approved, call at least 1 business day in advance to request a ride. Rides are scheduled using the Ecolane app. Call and receive Ecolane login information
rows.notes: Duplicate medical transportation row with no address; same GoDurham ACCESS paratransit program confirmed.

rows.service: Offers curb-to-curb bus service. Door-to-door service available by request
rows.schedule: GoDurham ACCESS Office: Monday - Friday, 8:00am - 5:00pm. Durham Station: Monday - Saturday, 6:00am - 12:00am Sunday, 7:00am - 9:00pm
rows.eligibility: People age 60 and over or with disabilities who are unable to use a fixed-route bus. Stop must be located within 3/4 of a mile of a fixed-route bus stop. Also available to people who live in rural areas of Durham County or need work-related, medical, human services, government, or personal care trips
rows.apply: Visit website to download application or call to have 1 mailed. If applying based on disability, part of application requires an assessment from a licensed medical professional. Return application in person or by mail. If approved, call at least 1 business day in advance to request a ride. Rides are scheduled using the Ecolane app. Call and receive Ecolane login information
rows.notes: Duplicate medical transportation row with no address; same GoDurham ACCESS paratransit program confirmed.; City/County inferred from agency name (211 had no address)

rows.service: Offers home mortgage and mortgage refinancing loans to people unable to use traditional banking services
rows.eligibility: Open to all
rows.apply: Visit, apply online, or visit website to print and complete application to become a member. Once a member, call or visit for loan information
rows.documents: Photo ID and Social Security Card
rows.notes: Agency website appears dead/404 — info from 211 only; Duplicate-ish row with no address listed. Website returns 403. Could not verify.

rows.eligibility: Open to all

rows.service: Offers financial education through online tools, webinars, and 1-on-1 counseling. Offers information on building credit, creating a budget, and managing debt. Also offers pre-purchase homebuyer counseling and information on reverse mortgages and foreclosure prevention
rows.eligibility: Open to all
rows.apply: Visit, apply online, or visit website to print and complete application to become a member. Once a member, call for more information
rows.notes: Agency website appears dead/404 — info from 211 only; Duplicate-ish row with no address listed. Website returns 403. Could not verify.

rows.agency: VOLUNTEERS OF AMERICA CHESAPEAKE AND CAROLINAS
rows.service: Offers permanent supportive housing to veterans with special needs. Residents receive case management services and referrals to other community resources
rows.eligibility: Veterans at-risk of homelessness or veterans experiencing homelessness who meet income limits. Enrolled in the HUD-VASH voucher program. Does not accept veterans that have been dishonorably discharged or are sex offenders
rows.apply: Call, email, or visit website for more information. There is often a waitlist
rows.documents: Photo ID, Social Security Card, DD214, and proof of income. Must have HUD-VASH Voucher
rows.notes: Site lists 'Maple Court Veteran Apartments' at 207 Commons Blvd, Durham 27704, phone 919-477-0571 — matches 211. HUD-VASH not named explicitly but program type matches.

rows.agency: DEPARTMENT OF SOCIAL SERVICES - DURHAM COUNTY
rows.service: Offers temporary financial help for people in a crisis. Crisis Intervention Program (CIP): Offers financial help with utility bills for heating or cooling emergencies. Payments are made to the utility provider. Help is available more than once every 12 months. Other financial help may be available based on circumstances. May help with rent or utilities, offer prescription assistance, bus passes, burial assistance, and more. Limited funding available
rows.eligibility: Crisis Intervention Program: Meet income limits and be at risk of a health-related emergency if utilities are shut off. May require a utility shut-off notice to be eligible. Requirements for other financial help vary
rows.apply: Call to see if funds are available and complete an application over the phone. May also visit to complete an application or call to have 1 mailed. A certain amount of appointments are available each day for rental assistance, first come first serve. Call as early as possible to reserve a slot
rows.documents: Proof of crisis, photo ID, birth certificate, proof of household income, copy of utility bill or payment receipt, and the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household. For rental assistance, an eviction notice, court notice, or late notice
rows.notes: DSS hours per agency website (verified 2026-05-01): close 5:30pm, not 5:00pm. Crisis Services under Aging & Adult Services; CIP/rent help is appointment-based and limited - call 919-560-8000 early

rows.service: Offers services for military members and their families. Offers financial help with rent, mortgage, utilities, and prescription medications. Also offers financial help with transportation, medical care, car repairs, and more. Limited funding available
rows.eligibility: Service members of rank E-1 to E-6 with DEERS eligible dependents currently deployed overseas and receiving Hostile Fire Pay and service members who actively served in the military after 9/11/2001 and suffered a wound, illness, or injury in the line of duty. Call or visit website for more information
rows.apply: Apply online within the first 10 days of the month. Applicants can reapply the following month if not approved
rows.documents: DD214 or copy of deployment orders, proof of wound, illness, or injury in the line of duty if applicable, and proof of household income
rows.notes: Detailed eligibility (rank E-1 to E-6, DEERS, deployed overseas, Hostile Fire Pay) per 211 - recommend confirming with agency before referral. National program; HQ in San Antonio TX, no Durham office; toll-free 877-264-3968 matches

rows.agency: SALVATION ARMY - DURHAM, ORANGE, AND PERSON COUNTIES
rows.service: Offers emergency financial help with rent, electric and heating utilities. Not able to help with deposits. May offer household goods for people involved in natural disasters or house fires. Funding may not always be available
rows.eligibility: Meet income limits and be in a crisis situation. Utilities must have final disconnect notice. Financial help can only be given once every 12 months. Priority is given to homes with minors or people with health conditions
rows.apply: Call and leave a message with name and phone number. Seen by appointment only
rows.documents: Photo ID, proof of household income, copies of monthly bills or receipts, and a final disconnect notice
rows.notes: Address 909 Liberty St confirmed. Site references 'Pathway of Hope' for rent/utility help but does not list specific hours, eligibility, or local direct number; 211 detail not contradicted.

rows.agency: VOLUNTEERS OF AMERICA CHESAPEAKE AND CAROLINAS
rows.service: Offers services to veterans and their families experiencing homelessness. Offers outreach, case management, and help getting VA and other public benefits. Also offers short-term financial help with rent, utilities, moving companies, and child care providers
rows.eligibility: A Veteran or a member of a family in which the head of household, or spouse of the head of household is a veteran with any discharge except Dishonorable. Must meet income limits and experiencing homelessness or at risk of becoming homeless
rows.documents: Social Security Card, photo ID, DD214, and proof of income
rows.notes: SSVF (Supportive Services for Veteran Families) address and phone per agency website (verified 2026-05-01); 211's 910-920-2994 number appears outdated

rows.service: Offers vouchers for approved Section 8 properties. Vouchers offer financial help with rent
rows.eligibility: Meet income limits and pass a background check
rows.apply: Call for waitlist information. Apply online at Online Pre-Application (https://ncdurham.tenmast.com/apply/)
rows.documents: Photo ID, Social Security card, birth certificate, and proof of income
rows.notes: Office address per agency website (verified 2026-05-01): 423 Morris Street, not 330 East Main. Online pre-application/waitlist status checking confirmed; waitlists may be closed

rows.service: Offers a weekly shuttle to and from Walmart at Glenn View Station. Serves different communities each day of the week: Monday : Veranda at Whitted School and Forest Hill Heights Tuesday : J.J. Henderson and Morehead Hills Wednesday : Preiss-Steele and Carver Creek Thursday : Morning Glory and Hosiery Mill Friday : Scattered Site - 300 Gary Street and 519 East Main Street
rows.schedule: Regional Information Center Hours: Monday - Sunday, 6:00am - 9:00pm. Senior Shuttle Hours of Operation: Monday - Friday, 10:00am - 3:05pm. For information on the location of the bus stop and scheduled departure times, visit website and download the community flyer
rows.eligibility: Older adults living in communities the shuttle service is available
rows.apply: Visit website to find bus stop and schedule times. No registration needed
rows.notes: GoDurham ACCESS senior shuttle program confirmed on site. Specific community schedule (Mon-Fri rotating Veranda/Henderson/Preiss-Steele/Morning Glory/Scattered Site) not detailed on homepage; download community flyer per 211 instruction.

rows.service: Offers a shelter for men experiencing homelessness
rows.schedule: Mon 7:00am - 9:00pm; Tue 7:00am - 9:00pm; Wed 7:00am - 9:00pm; Thu 7:00am - 9:00pm; Fri 7:00am - 9:00pm; Sat 7:00am - 9:00pm; Sun 7:00am - 9:00pm;
rows.eligibility: Adult men experiencing homelessness. No sex offender of any type (pending, charged, registered and/or any other sex related offenses) will be allowed to check in
rows.documents: Photo ID or a printout of a police/sheriff's criminal history report
rows.notes: Good Samaritan Inn (women/children) confirmed; children up to age 12 with mother per site - matches 211. Sex offender restriction confirmed. Specific 7-day on-campus and 1-year vehicle restriction not detailed on site but consistent with policy framework.

rows.service: Offers a shelter for single women and women with children experiencing homelessness
rows.schedule: Mon 7:00am - 9:00pm; Tue 7:00am - 9:00pm; Wed 7:00am - 9:00pm; Thu 7:00am - 9:00pm; Fri 7:00am - 9:00pm; Sat 7:00am - 9:00pm; Sun 7:00am - 9:00pm;
rows.eligibility: Single women or mothers with children age 12 and under who are experiencing homelessness. No sex offender of any type (pending, charged, registered and/or any other sex related offenses) will be allowed to check in. Must agree to stay on campus for first 7 days. Vehicles are not allowed for the first year
rows.documents: Photo ID or a printout of a police/sheriff's criminal history report
rows.notes: Good Samaritan Inn (women/children) confirmed; children up to age 12 with mother per site - matches 211. Sex offender restriction confirmed. Specific 7-day on-campus and 1-year vehicle restriction not detailed on site but consistent with policy framework.

rows.service: Offers referrals to homeless shelters in the area. CE may also offer information and referrals to other resources such as health care, transportation, and more. People MUST go through Coordinated Entry to get access to a shelter
rows.schedule: Durham Entry Point - Phone Services : Monday - Friday, 8:00am - 5:00pm Saturday, Sunday or holidays, 4:00pm - 8:00pm. Durham Entry Point - In-Person Intake at Aging and Adult Services : Monday - Friday, 8:00am - 5:00pm
rows.eligibility: Open to all
rows.notes: Agency website does not mention this program — info from 211 only; Page confirms Coordinated Entry concept but has no Durham Entry Point hours, phone, or address. 211 data is the only operational source; recommend confirming with Durham Entry Point directly.

rows.service: Offers 3 meals a day
rows.schedule: Monday - Friday: Breakfast, 8:00am - 9:00am Can pick up a bagged to-go lunch during breakfast time Dinner, 6:00pm - 7:00pm. Saturday, Sunday, and Holidays: Breakfast, 9:30am - 10:30am Lunch, 12:30pm - 1:30pm Dinner, 6:00pm - 7:00pm
rows.eligibility: Open to all
rows.notes: Agency website does not mention this program — info from 211 only; Site confirms Community Cafe serves '3 free meals per day, 7 days a week, 365 days per year' — consistent with 211 schedule. No more granular meal-time info on homepage.

rows.agency: DEPARTMENT OF SOCIAL SERVICES - DURHAM COUNTY
rows.service: Offers financial help with property tax. Does not help with past due tax bills, only the current tax bill. Payment goes directly to vendor
rows.eligibility: The home must be the primary and only residence. Must have owned the home for at least 5 years. Must meet income limits and not receiving tax deferment or other state subsidy property tax help
rows.apply: Apply online or call to schedule an appointment
rows.documents: Photo ID, copy of tax bill showing name, and proof of household income
rows.notes: DSS hours per agency website (verified 2026-05-01): close 5:30pm, not 5:00pm. Property Tax Relief not specifically named; site lists 'Low-Income Homeowner's Relief (LIHR)' which appears related

rows.service: Sells donated clothes and household items like bedding, appliances, furniture, and more
rows.eligibility: Open to all
rows.notes: Agency website does not mention this program — info from 211 only; Agency website does not mention a thrift shop or the Chapel Hill Blvd location; using 211 data only. Worth a phone confirmation before referral.

rows.service: Offers an online service to connect individuals to nearby commuters who have similar schedules
rows.eligibility: Open to all
rows.notes: Confirmed online carpool-matching platform (RideShark solution). 24/7 access via website and iOS/Android apps consistent with 211's all-hours listing. Not a ride service — it matches commuters with similar routes.

rows.agency: DEPARTMENT OF SOCIAL SERVICES - DURHAM COUNTY
rows.service: Offers financial help with heating bills once every 12 months. Payments are based on household size, income, and heating source. Payments are made to the vendor. People do not need to be in a crisis to get help
rows.eligibility: Meet income limits and resource guidelines. Each household is only eligible once, even if multiple members apply
rows.apply: Call to see if funds are available. Visit to complete an application, call to have 1 mailed, or apply online at epass.nc.gov (https://epass.nc.gov/). NOTE: Households with a person age 60 and over or with a person under age 60 who is receiving services from the NC Division of Aging and Adult Services can apply beginning December 1. All other households can apply beginning January 1
rows.documents: Photo ID, birth certificate, proof of household income, copy of utility bill, latest bank statement, the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household, and proof of disability if under age 60
rows.notes: DSS hours per agency website (verified 2026-05-01): close 5:30pm, not 5:00pm. LIEAP not detailed on main DSS page; seasonal eligibility (60+ from Dec 1, all from Jan 1) per 211 not contradicted

rows.service: Offers emergency financial help for electric utilities, prescription medications, and heating fuel. Offers financial help with transportation expenses for medical appointments outside of Edenton city limits. Also offers a clothing closet. Also offers 1-day hotel vouchers for people escaping domestic violence or for people who have experienced a house fire and cannot return to the home
rows.schedule: Emergency Financial Help: Message can be left any time of the day. Clothing Closet: Tuesday - Thursday, 9:00am - 3:00pm
rows.eligibility: Utilities: Accepted on a case-by-case basis. Must not have received help in the last 6 months from another organization. Available once every 12 months. Prescription: Uninsured. Clothing Closet: Open to all. Transportation: Accepted on a case-by-case basis. Must have regular medical appointments outside the city limits of Edenton such as radiation or chemotherapy
rows.apply: Emergency Financial Help: Call. Phone is checked throughout the day for messages. 1 Day Hotel Voucher: For domestic violence, contact the local police department to receive a referral. For house fires, call. Clothing Closet: Visit the office
rows.notes: No website provided in batch input; unable to verify against agency site.

rows.agency: DEPARTMENT OF SOCIAL SERVICES - CHOWAN COUNTY
rows.service: Special Assistance offers a cash supplement to help pay for room and board in an adult care home or group home. Special Assistance In-Home offers a cash supplement to help people who are at risk of entering a residential facility but prefer to live at home. Long Term Care offers a cash supplement to help pay for room and board in a long term care or skilled nursing facility. Payments are made to the vendor
rows.eligibility: Meet income limits, be eligible for an residential or skilled nursing facility, and have a disability or be age 65 and over
rows.apply: Special Assistance Programs: Visit to complete an application or call to have it mailed. Long Term Care: Visit to complete an application, call to have it mailed, or apply online at epass.nc.gov (https://epass.nc.gov/)

rows.documents: Proof of address and proof of income
rows.notes: Site confirms address and phone; specific hours not on homepage.

rows.service: Offers emergency financial assistance for rent and utilities. Not able to help with deposits. Payment goes directly to the vendor. Offers an onsite food pantry
rows.eligibility: Residents of Gastonia in a financial crisis. Crisis can include job loss, pay reduction, and unexpected bills. Financial assistance may be applied for once every 30 days, depending on individual situation. Call for more information. Food pantry is available once every 30 - 90 days, depending on individual situation and food supply
rows.apply: Apply online at crisisassistancegastonia.force.com/application/s/ (https://crisisassistancegastonia.force.com/application/s/). Applications are only accepted online Monday at 9:00am until Thursday at 2:00pm. People can call if there is no access to a computer or internet or they need help completing the online form
rows.documents: Photo ID, proof of address, proof of income for everyone in the household. Proof of expenses if an unexpected bill is the reason for crisis, and utility disconnect notice or late rent/first rent notice

rows.service: Offers a drive-thru food pantry, mobile family medicine services, shower facilities, and laundry services
rows.eligibility: Open to all
rows.apply: Call or visit website for more information
rows.notes: Site lists Bountiful Blessings pantry phone as 704-468-0135; prior 211 phone was 704-864-8616.

rows.service: Offers food pantry and clothing closet
rows.schedule: Food: Tuesday and Thursday, 9:00am - 11:00am. Clothing: Wednesday, 9:00am - 11:00am
rows.eligibility: Open to all
rows.documents: Photo ID for person applying and Social Security cards or birth certificates for everyone in the household
rows.notes: Site redirects to salvationarmyusa.org/nc/gastonia/union-rd-corps; site lists corps phone 704-867-6910 (211 had 704-867-6145); site does not show food pantry/clothing closet specific hours.

rows.agency: THE SALVATION ARMY OF GASTON AND LINCOLN COUNTIES

rows.eligibility: Open to all

rows.notes: Site redirects to salvationarmyusa.org/nc/gastonia/union-rd-corps; corps phone listed as 704-867-6910 vs prior 211 704-867-6145.

rows.service: Offers major home repairs. Repairs may include plumbing, heating, or water and sewer connection, etc
rows.eligibility: Homeowners who meet income limits. Must not have received financial help through City of Gastonia within the past 5 - 10 years. Must be current on property taxes and mortgage. Priority is given to homeowners with disability, larger families, and people over age 62
rows.documents: Proof of household income, electric/heating fuel bills for the last 12 months, and proof of homeownership or a permission letter from landlord
rows.notes: Site redirects to gastonianc.gov; Housing & Community Engagement at 181 S South St; main line 704-866-6714.

rows.service: Offers homebuyer education
rows.eligibility: Open to all
rows.notes: Site redirects to gastonianc.gov; Housing & Community Engagement at 181 S South St; main line 704-866-6714.

rows.service: Offers rental properties with rent based on income
rows.eligibility: Meet income limits and pass a background check
rows.apply: Call or visit website for waitlist information. Visit website to download application and print. Return completed forms to office in person, by mail, by fax, or scan and email
rows.documents: Photo ID, Social Security card, birth certificate, and proof of income
rows.notes: Site lists hours 9am-4pm Mon-Thu (closed Fri); main phone 704-864-6771 differs from 211.

rows.service: Offers homebuyer education, credit counseling, and financial help with housing down payments
rows.eligibility: Meet income limits
rows.apply: Visit website to download application or call to have one mailed. Call to schedule an appointment
rows.documents: Photo ID, Social Security card, last 2 years of W-2's and tax returns, 2 months of pay stubs and other income, and 2 months of bank statements
rows.notes: Site redirects to gastonianc.gov; Housing & Community Engagement Department at 181 S South St; HUD Administrator direct 704-866-6756.

rows.service: Offers education and resource referral for women with postpartum depression and need to be screened or referred to a professional. Helps mothers recognize symptoms of postpartum depression. Also offers supplies such as diapers, blankets, mother supplies, infant formula in emergency cases such as fire, displacement, or domestic violence
rows.eligibility: Mothers of a newborn and postpartum depression or victim of displacement, fire, and domestic violence
rows.documents: Documents for the newborn, referral form, and consent form to receive help
rows.notes: Site lists Suite 100 in address; maternal mental health/postpartum services confirmed.

rows.agency: DEPARTMENT OF SOCIAL SERVICES - GASTON COUNTY
rows.service: Special Assistance offers a cash supplement to help pay for room and board in an adult care home or group home. Special Assistance In-Home offers a cash supplement to help people who are at risk of entering a residential facility but prefer to live at home. Long Term Care offers a cash supplement to help pay for room and board in a long term care or skilled nursing facility. Payments are made to the vendor
rows.eligibility: Meet income limits, be eligible for an approved residential or skilled nursing facility, and have a disability or be age 65 and over. Must be a US citizen or eligible alien
rows.apply: Special Assistance Programs: Visit to complete an application or call to have it mailed. Long Term Care: Visit to complete an application, call to have it mailed, or apply online at epass.nc.gov (https://epass.nc.gov/)
rows.documents: Special Assistance Programs: Last 3 years of bank statements, proof of household income and resources, proof of monthly expenses, and the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household. Long Term Care: Last 5 years of bank statements, an FL-2 form from a doctor, proof of household income and resources, proof of monthly expenses, and the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household
rows.address: 330 Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. Way

rows.agency: DEPARTMENT OF SOCIAL SERVICES - GASTON COUNTY
rows.service: Offers health insurance for people with low income and limited resources. Medicaid transportation is available to Medicaid recipients who need assistance getting to Medicaid-approved medical appointments and providers
rows.eligibility: Medicaid : Eligibility based on age, pregnancy status, disability status, income, and resources. Must be a US citizen or eligible alien. Medicaid Transportation: People already receiving Medicaid through Medicaid Direct or the Eastern Band of Cherokee Indians tribal health plan. People already receiving Medicaid through a different health plan (AmeriHealth Caritas, Carolina Complete Health, HealthyBlue, UnitedHealthcare, or WellCare) must contact their plan provider for transportation
rows.apply: Medicaid : Visit to complete an application, call to have one mailed, or apply online at epass.nc.gov (https://epass.nc.gov/). Medicaid Transportation: Call to schedule medical transportation
rows.documents: Proof of household income and resources, proof of monthly expenses, and the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household
rows.address: 330 Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. Way

rows.service: Offers support services for cancer patients and their family. Offers liquid nutrition, medication assistance, medical equipment, transportation assistance, and more
rows.eligibility: Must have a cancer diagnosis and live in Gaston County

rows.service: Offers monthly financial help for electric bills for 1 year
rows.eligibility: Must be a City of Gastonia Electric customer for at least 12 months and meet income limits. This program will not assist with a currently-due bill or a past-due bill. Cannot help residents whose electric utilities are included with their rent or move within 12 months of receiving financial help. Utility bill must be in the person's name that is applying for assistance. Must re-certify every year
rows.documents: Proof of income, most recent utility statement, photo ID, most recent bank statement, and most recent lease agreement, verification or mortgage, and/or other recent proof of residency
rows.notes: Site redirects from cityofgastonia.com to gastonianc.gov; main City Hall is 181 S South St 28052, main line 704-866-6714; 150 S York may be old address.

I-Ride Customer Service: Monday - Friday, 7:00am - 7:00pm Saturday, Sunday, 9:00am - 5:00pm. I-Ride Service Hours: Monday - Friday, 5:30am - 10:00pm Saturday, Sunday, 7:30am - 7:30pm

rows.service: Provides essential home repairs that make a home warmer, drier, and safer - not cosmetic upgrades. Services include critical repairs such as roof and flooring repair, plumbing and electrical hazard mitigation, and other health- and safety-related fixes. Also offers energy-efficiency improvements and accessibility modifications, including wheelchair ramps and other home adaptations
rows.eligibility: Income below 80% of the Area Median Income for Guilford County, and have owned and occupied the home for at least 5 years. May not own additional properties
rows.apply: Call or visit website to submit a repair request
rows.documents: Proof of address and proof of income

rows.service: Supports older adult homeowners with a combination of in-home healthcare visits and essential home safety improvements to help them live independently in their own homes for longer. Participants receive at least four in-home occupational therapist visits and four registered nurse visits provided by Cone Health at no cost to the homeowner. These visits focus on safety education, fall-prevention strategies, and personalized recommendations. Provides repairs and accessibility modifications, such as access ramps, grab bars, and other safety upgrades
rows.eligibility: Older adults and people with disabilities, age 60 or older, whose income is below 80% of the Area Median Income for Guilford County. Must own and live in the home that needs repairs. Must commit to 4 months of visits with a nurse and/or therapist and meet Cone Health guidelines for program
rows.documents: Proof of home ownership and proof of income

rows.agency: DEPARTMENT OF SOCIAL SERVICES - GUILFORD COUNTY
rows.service: Special Assistance offers a cash supplement to help pay for room and board in an adult care home or group home. Special Assistance In-Home offers a cash supplement to help people who are at risk of entering a residential facility but prefer to live at home. Long Term Care offers a cash supplement to help pay for room and board in a long term care or skilled nursing facility. Payments are made to the vendor
rows.eligibility: Meet income limits, live in an approved residential or skilled nursing facility, and have a disability or be age 65 and over
rows.apply: Special Assistance: Visit to complete an application or call to have it mailed. Long Term Care: Visit to complete an application, call to have it mailed, or apply online at epass.nc.gov (https://epass.nc.gov/)
rows.documents: Special Assistance: Last 3 years of bank statements, proof of household income and resources, proof of monthly expenses, and the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household. Long Term Care: Last 5 years of bank statements, an FL-2 form from a doctor, proof of household income and resources, proof of monthly expenses, and the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household
rows.notes: Site lists main office at 301 W Market St Greensboro; 211 had 1203 Maple Street.

Medicaid Transportation: People already receiving Medicaid through Medicaid Direct or the Eastern Band of Cherokee Indians tribal health plan. People already receiving Medicaid through a different health plan (AmeriHealth Caritas, Carolina Complete Health, HealthyBlue, UnitedHealthcare, or WellCare) must contact their plan provider for transportation
rows.apply: Medicaid : Visit to complete an application, call to have 1 mailed, or apply online at epass.nc.gov (https://epass.nc.gov/). Medicaid Transportation: Call to schedule medical transportation
rows.documents: Proof of household income and resources, proof of monthly expenses, and the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household
rows.notes: Site lists main office at 301 W Market St Greensboro; 211 had 1203 Maple Street (alternate location).

rows.agency: UNITED STATES DEPARTMENT OF HOUSING AND URBAN DEVELOPMENT
rows.service: Administers the Section 8 Housing Choice Voucher Program and other HUD-sponsored housing programs. Offers home loans through the Federal Housing Administration (FHA). Works to ensure fair lending and fight housing discrimination through the Office of Fair Housing and Equal Opportunity (FHEO). Investigates complaints about housing discrimination
rows.eligibility: Open to all
rows.apply: General Questions: Call Regional Office or email NC_Webmanager@hud.gov. Section 8 and Other Rental Programs: Call the Main Office or email HUD-PIHRC@tngusa.net. FHA Loans or Programs: Call the FHA Resource Center or email answers@hud.gov. Report Housing Discrimination: Call, visit website to download form and return by mail to the Regional FHEO Office, or email form to NC_Webmanager@hud.gov. Visit hud.gov/fairhousing/fileacomplaint (https://www.hud.gov/program_offices/fair_housing_equal_opp/online-complaint) to submit report online
rows.notes: HUD national homepage operational; specific Greensboro field office details not on homepage.

rows.agency: DEPARTMENT OF SOCIAL SERVICES - PITT COUNTY
rows.service: Offers temporary financial help for people in a crisis. Crisis Intervention Program (CIP) : Offers financial help with utility bills for heating or cooling emergencies. Payments are made to the utility provider. Help is available more than once every 12 months. Other financial help may be available for rent, based on circumstances. Limited funds available
rows.eligibility: Crisis Intervention Program: Meet income limits and be at risk of a health-related emergency if utilities are shut off. May require a utility shut-off notice to be eligible. Must be a US citizen or eligible alien. Rent help is available to renters with an eviction notice, requirements for other financial help may vary

rows.documents: Proof of crisis, photo ID, birth certificate, proof of household income, copy of utility bill or payment receipt, and the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household, and eviction notice if applying for rent assistance

rows.agency: DEPARTMENT OF SOCIAL SERVICES - VANCE COUNTY
rows.service: Offers financial help with heating bills once every 12 months. Payments are based on household size, income, and heating source. Payments are made to the utility provider. People do not need to be in a crisis to get help
rows.eligibility: Meet income limits and resource guidelines. Each household is only eligible once, even if multiple members apply. Must be a US citizen or eligible alien
rows.apply: Call to see if funds are available. Visit to complete an application, call to have 1 mailed, or apply online at epass.nc.gov (https://epass.nc.gov/). NOTE: Households with a person age 60 and over or with a person under age 60 who is receiving services from the NC Division of Aging and Adult Services can apply beginning December 1. All other households can apply beginning January 1
rows.documents: Photo ID, birth certificate, proof of household income, copy of utility bill, latest bank statement, the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household, and proof of disability if under age 60

rows.agency: DEPARTMENT OF SOCIAL SERVICES - HENDERSON COUNTY
rows.service: Special Assistance offers a cash supplement to help pay for room and board in an adult care home or group home. Special Assistance In-Home offers a cash supplement to help people who are at risk of entering a residential facility but prefer to live at home. Long Term Care offers a cash supplement to help pay for room and board in a long term care or skilled nursing facility. Payments are made to the vendor
rows.eligibility: Meet income limits, be eligible for an approved residential or skilled nursing facility, and have a disability or be age 65 and over
rows.apply: Special Assistance Programs: Visit to complete an application or call to have it mailed. Long Term Care: Visit to complete an application, call to have it mailed, or apply online at epass.nc.gov (https://epass.nc.gov/)
rows.documents: Special Assistance Programs: Last 3 years of bank statements, proof of household income and resources, proof of monthly expenses, and the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household. Long Term Care: Last 5 years of bank statements, an FL-2 form from a doctor, proof of household income and resources, proof of monthly expenses, and the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household

rows.service: Offers healthy meals, meal kits, and food markets to people in the community. Works with local farms and health partners to help neighbors get fresh, healthy food. The goal is to support health and care through food
rows.eligibility: Open to all

rows.agency: DEPARTMENT OF SOCIAL SERVICES - HENDERSON COUNTY
rows.service: Offers health insurance for people with low income and limited resources. Medicaid transportation is available to Medicaid recipients who need assistance getting to Medicaid-approved medical appointments and providers
rows.eligibility: Medicaid : Eligibility based on age, pregnancy status, disability status, income, and resources. Medicaid Transportation: People already receiving Medicaid through Medicaid Direct or the Eastern Band of Cherokee Indians tribal health plan. People already receiving Medicaid through a different health plan (AmeriHealth Caritas, Carolina Complete Health, HealthyBlue, UnitedHealthcare, or WellCare) must contact their plan provider for transportation
rows.apply: Medicaid: Visit to complete an application, call to have it mailed, or apply online at epass.nc.gov (https://epass.nc.gov/). Medicaid Transportation: Call to schedule medical transportation
rows.documents: Proof of household income and resources, proof of monthly expenses, and the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household

rows.service: Offers door-to-door bus service to congregate meals, non-emergency medical appointments, shopping centers, job sites, and more
rows.eligibility: Medical Appointments: Open to all. Employment Sites: People with disabilities. Shopping: Age 60 and over. Some programs may have more specific eligibility requirements
rows.apply: Call 2 - 3 weeks in advance for local appointments. Call 3 - 4 weeks in advance for appointments outside of the county
rows.notes: Site lists 220 King Creek Blvd, Hendersonville 28793 (PO Box 685); 211 had 526 7th Avenue East.

rows.service: Offers an animal shelter with adoptable dogs, cats, and small animals. Some animals may be available to foster. Offers low-cost spay and neuter services and vaccinations, and financial help with veterinary expenses. Also offers support and advice through a Pet Helpline, and a pet loss support group. Offers a limited supply of pet food in emergency situations. Offers pet food, support, and other services to pets living with a Meals on Wheels recipient. Offers a re-homing listing for those who need to re-home their pet but would like to keep them out of a shelter. Offers temporary boarding when owner is unable to keep animal due to relocation or an emergency. Also offers financial help with one-time pet deposits for rental units
rows.eligibility: Open to all. Some programs may have more specific requirements. Renters must have landlord's permission to adopt a pet
rows.documents: None needed to visit. Photo ID and proof of address needed for other services
rows.notes: Specific facility hours/locations on subpage; main phone confirmed.

rows.service: Offers baby diapers, wipes, and formula

rows.service: Offers temporary, emergency night shelter. Residents must be out during the day looking for work and housing through the referrals given to them. Space is limited. Cold Weather Shelter is available when temperatures reach 32 degrees. The same rules as the regular shelter apply. Extreme Cold Weather Shelter is available when temperatures reach below 20 degrees. This shelter is open to everyone but cannot help with pets
rows.schedule: Mon 8:30am - 4:00pm; Tue 8:30am - 4:00pm; Wed 8:30am - 4:00pm; Thu 8:30am - 4:00pm; Fri 8:30am - 4:00pm; Sat 8:30am - 4:00pm; Sun 8:30am - 4:00pm;
rows.eligibility: Age 18 and over, or accompanied by a parent. Residents must be sober and not under the influence of drugs. Cannot accommodate pets
rows.apply: Call ahead to schedule an admissions interview Monday - Friday, 12:00pm - 4:00pm
rows.documents: Proof of ID and address in Henderson, Polk, or Transylvania counties. Minors also need proof of ID and guardianship, such as a birth certificate
rows.notes: Homepage does not detail emergency night shelter intake hours.

rows.service: Offers emergency financial help with rent and utilities, with eviction or disconnection notice. Also offers assistance with heating fuel, up to 100 gallons per home (no portable containers). Provides prescription medication vouchers (no controlled medications), and gasoline vouchers up to $50 for medical appointments or jobs. For complete details and days/times services are available, go to www.iam-hc.org/get-help (https://www.iam-hc.org/get-help)
rows.eligibility: Henderson County residents who meet income limits and are experiencing a financial crisis
rows.apply: Call or visit for more information
rows.documents: Utility Assistance : Photo ID or Social Security card, proof of address, and copy of past-due utility bill or disconnection notice. Must visit at least 2 days before cut-off date. Must arrive before 11:00 am. Rent Assistance : Photo ID or Social Security card, proof of address, proof of household income from past 2 months, and eviction notice. See website for a complete list of required materials. Gasoline Vouchers : Proof of medical appointment, job interview, or new hire date

rows.agency: DEPARTMENT OF SOCIAL SERVICES - CATAWBA COUNTY
rows.service: Special Assistance offers a cash supplement to help pay for room and board in an adult care home or group home. Special Assistance In-Home offers a cash supplement to help people who are at risk of entering a residential facility but prefer to live at home. Long Term Care offers a cash supplement to help pay for room and board in a long term care or skilled nursing facility. Payments are made to the vendor
rows.eligibility: Meet income limits, be eligible for an approved residential or skilled nursing facility, and have a disability or be age 65 and over. Must be a US citizen or eligible alien
rows.apply: Special Assistance Programs: Visit to complete an application or call to have it mailed. Long Term Care: Visit to complete an application, call to have it mailed, or apply online at https://epass.nc.gov/ (https://epass.nc.gov/)
rows.documents: Special Assistance: Last 3 years of bank statements, proof of household income and resources, proof of monthly expenses, and the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household. Long Term Care: Last 5 years of bank statements, an FL-2 form from a doctor, proof of household income and resources, proof of monthly expenses, and the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household

rows.agency: DEPARTMENT OF SOCIAL SERVICES - CATAWBA COUNTY
rows.service: Offers health insurance for people with low income and limited resources. Medicaid transportation is available to Medicaid recipients who need assistance getting to Medicaid-approved medical appointments and providers
rows.eligibility: Medicaid : Eligibility based on age, pregnancy status, disability status, income, and resources. Must be a US citizen or eligible alien. Medicaid Transportation: People already receiving Medicaid through Medicaid Direct or the Eastern Band of Cherokee Indians tribal health plan. People already receiving Medicaid through a different health plan (AmeriHealth Caritas, Carolina Complete Health, HealthyBlue, UnitedHealthcare, or WellCare) must contact their plan provider for transportation
rows.apply: Medicaid : Visit to complete an application, call to have 1 mailed, or apply online at epass.nc.gov (https://epass.nc.gov/). Medicaid Transportation: Call to schedule medical transportation
rows.documents: Proof of household income and resources, proof of monthly expenses, and the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household

rows.service: Provides a free pantry with personal hygiene and household cleaning items to assist women and families facing urgent needs due to circumstances such as job loss or fleeing domestic violence. Standard Emergency Kits are available once per year per household and are subject to eligibility. Please call the front desk (tel:828-322-6333) to complete a screening. Welcome Home Kits contain essential household items such as towels, brooms, and trash bins to help women transitioning from homelessness into stable housing. These kits are available by referral only from a partner agency that can verify the family's current housing situation. Menstrual Hygiene Products - No Strings Attached provides free menstrual hygiene products-no forms, no questions asked. Supplies are available 24/7 for self-service pickup from the front porch
rows.eligibility: Women and families experiencing homelessness
