rows.eligibility: Utility Assistance: Meet income limits with past due utility bill, and have a documentable crisis in the past 6 months or be on a fixed income. Rent Assistance: Meet income limits with past due rent and be over age 65 or have children under age 18 in the home. Must have a documentable crisis in the past 2 months or be on a fixed income. People with subsidized housing or other forms of rent assistance are not eligible
rows.apply: Call Monday morning between 9:00am and 12:00pm to apply and schedule an appointment. Must speak with a person to apply. Voicemails may not be answered and will not be considered as an application
rows.documents: Photo ID or proof of address, proof of income for everyone in the household, proof of monthly expenses, and proof of crisis

rows.service: Special Assistance offers a cash supplement to help pay for room and board in an adult care home or group home. Long Term Care offers a cash supplement to help pay for room and board in a long term care or skilled nursing facility. Payments are made to the vendor
rows.eligibility: Meet income limits, be eligible for an approved residential or skilled nursing facility, and have a disability or be age 65 and over. Must be a US citizen or eligible alien
rows.apply: Special Assistance Programs: Visit to complete an application or call to have it mailed. Long Term Care: Visit to complete an application, call to have it mailed, or apply online at epass.nc.gov (https://epass.nc.gov/)
rows.documents: Special Assistance Programs: Last 3 years of bank statements, proof of household income and resources, proof of monthly expenses, and the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household. Long Term Care: Last 5 years of bank statements, an FL-2 form from a doctor, proof of household income and resources, proof of monthly expenses, and the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household

rows.agency: DEPARTMENT OF SOCIAL SERVICES - DUPLIN COUNTY
rows.service: Offers health insurance for people with low income and limited resources. Medicaid transportation is available to Medicaid recipients who need assistance getting to Medicaid-approved medical appointments and providers
rows.eligibility: Medicaid : Eligibility based on age, pregnancy status, disability status, income, and resources. Medicaid Transportation: People already receiving Medicaid through Medicaid Direct or the Eastern Band of Cherokee Indians tribal health plan. People already receiving Medicaid through a different health plan (AmeriHealth Caritas, Carolina Complete Health, HealthyBlue, UnitedHealthcare, or WellCare) must contact their plan provider for transportation
rows.apply: Medicaid : Visit to complete an application, call to have one mailed, or apply online at epass.nc.gov (https://epass.nc.gov/). Medicaid Transportation: Call to schedule medical transportation
rows.documents: Proof of household income and resources, proof of monthly expenses, and the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household

rows.agency: DEPARTMENT OF SOCIAL SERVICES - CALDWELL COUNTY
rows.service: Special Assistance offers a cash supplement to help pay for room and board in an adult care home or group home. Special Assistance In-Home offers a cash supplement to help people who are at risk of entering a residential facility but prefer to live at home. Long Term Care offers a cash supplement to help pay for room and board in a long term care or skilled nursing facility. Payments are made to the vendor
rows.eligibility: Meet income limits, live in an approved residential or skilled nursing facility, and have a disability or be age 65 and over
rows.apply: Special Assistance Programs: Visit to complete an application or call to have it mailed. Long Term Care: Visit to complete an application, call to have it mailed, or apply online at epass.nc.gov (https://epass.nc.gov/)
rows.documents: Special Assistance: Last 3 years of bank statements, proof of household income and resources, proof of monthly expenses, and the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household. Long Term Care: Last 5 years of bank statements, an FL-2 form from a doctor, proof of household income and resources, proof of monthly expenses, and the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household

rows.agency: DEPARTMENT OF SOCIAL SERVICES - CALDWELL COUNTY
rows.service: Offers health insurance for people with low income and limited resources. Medicaid transportation is available to Medicaid recipients who need assistance getting to Medicaid-approved medical appointments and providers
rows.eligibility: Medicaid : Eligibility based on age, pregnancy status, disability status, income, and resources. Medicaid Transportation: People already receiving Medicaid through Medicaid Direct or the Eastern Band of Cherokee Indians tribal health plan. People already receiving Medicaid through a different health plan (AmeriHealth Caritas, Carolina Complete Health, HealthyBlue, UnitedHealthcare, or WellCare) must contact their plan provider for transportation
rows.apply: Medicaid : Visit to complete an application, call to have one mailed, or apply online at epass.nc.gov (https://epass.nc.gov/). Medicaid Transportation: Call to schedule medical transportation
rows.documents: Proof of household income and resources, proof of monthly expenses, and the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household

Payments are made to the vendor. Help is available more than once every 12 months. Other financial help may be available for rent and prescription medication, based on circumstances. Limited funds available

rows.agency: DEPARTMENT OF SOCIAL SERVICES - LINCOLN COUNTY
rows.service: Special Assistance offers a cash supplement to help pay for room and board in an adult care home or group home. Special Assistance In-Home offers a cash supplement to help people who are at risk of entering a residential facility but prefer to live at home. Long Term Care offers a cash supplement to help pay for room and board in a long term care or skilled nursing facility. Payments are made to the vendor
rows.eligibility: Meet income limits, be eligible for an approved residential or skilled nursing facility, and have a disability or be age 65 and over. Must be a US citizen or eligible alien
rows.apply: Special Assistance Programs: Visit to complete an application or call to have it mailed. Long Term Care: Visit to complete an application, call to have it mailed, or apply online at epass.nc.gov (https://epass.nc.gov/)
rows.documents: Special Assistance Programs: Last 3 years of bank statements, proof of household income and resources, proof of monthly expenses, and the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household. Long Term Care: Last 5 years of bank statements, an FL-2 form from a doctor, proof of household income and resources, proof of monthly expenses, and the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household
rows.notes: Address confirmed at 1136 East Main Street; specific Long Term Care phone 704-732-1969 not detailed on main DSS page.

rows.service: Delivers meals to people who have no way to prepare meals and have serious health issues
rows.eligibility: Must have health condition that limits ability to cook and no one in the home to prepare meals
rows.notes: Main office 207 S Poplar St per site; meals delivery program confirmed.

rows.agency: DEPARTMENT OF SOCIAL SERVICES - LINCOLN COUNTY
rows.service: Offers health insurance for people with low income and limited resources. Medicaid transportation is available to Medicaid recipients who need assistance getting to Medicaid-approved medical appointments and providers
rows.eligibility: Medicaid: Eligibility based on age, pregnancy status, disability status, income, and resources. Must be a US citizen or eligible alien. Medicaid Transportation: People already receiving Medicaid through Medicaid Direct or the Eastern Band of Cherokee Indians tribal health plan. People already receiving Medicaid through a different health plan (AmeriHealth Caritas, Carolina Complete Health, HealthyBlue, UnitedHealthcare, or WellCare) must contact their plan provider for transportation
rows.apply: Medicaid : Visit to complete an application, call to have one mailed, or apply online at epass.nc.gov (https://epass.nc.gov/). Medicaid Transportation: Call to schedule medical transportation
rows.documents: Proof of household income and resources, proof of monthly expenses, and the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household
rows.notes: Site confirms DSS at 1136 East Main Street; current scam alert about fake Medicaid recertification texts.

NOTE: Households with a person age 60 and over or with a person under age 60 who is receiving services from the NC Division of Aging and Adult Services can apply beginning December 1. All other households can apply beginning January 1

rows.schedule: Mon 5:30pm - 8:00am; Tue 5:30pm - 8:00am; Wed 5:30pm - 8:00am; Thu 5:30pm - 8:00am; Fri 5:30pm - 8:00am; Sat 5:30pm - 8:00am; Sun 5:30pm - 8:00am;
rows.eligibility: Regular Shelter: Men, women, and children experiencing homelessness. Cannot be a sex offender, or under the influence of drugs or alcohol, and must be able to pass a background check and drug screen. Cold Night Shelter: Open to anyone who is homeless. Cannot be under the influence of drugs or alcohol
rows.apply: Visit the office. Interviews are done in-person only. Call for more information
rows.notes: Site shows shelter office hours 5:30pm-9:00pm; new guest intake 5:30-6:30pm; current guests 5:30pm-8:00am, consistent with 211 schedule.

rows.service: Provide families, men, and women with temporary overnight shelter. Offers "Code Purple" extreme weather shelter as well on cold nights. Extended stay is possible to an individual or family that demonstrates progress made toward their goals. Anyone desiring to stay at the shelter longer than 90 days must be recommended by the house manager and approved by a member of the oversight committee
rows.schedule: Mon 5:30pm - 9:00pm; Tue 5:30pm - 9:00pm; Wed 5:30pm - 9:00pm; Thu 5:30pm - 9:00pm; Fri 5:30pm - 9:00pm; Sat 5:30pm - 9:00pm; Sun 5:30pm - 9:00pm;
rows.eligibility: Open to all
rows.apply: Call or visit website for more information
rows.documents: A Photo Identification Must be able to pass a drug screen Be willing and able to do chores Pass a background check No sex offenders accepted at any time Must be willing to abide by all rules
rows.notes: Site lists phone 828-837-2654; 211 had 828-360-2064. Office hours 5:30-9:00pm align.

rows.service: Offers a community meal and a food pantry
rows.schedule: Community Meal: Monday, 10:30am - 12:30pm. Sharing Center Food Pantry: Monday and Friday, 12:00pm - 2:00pm
rows.eligibility: Registration is required upon first visit and must meet income guidelines and have proof of identity. The Sharing Center Food Bank is a group from several Churches in the area that provide food for those in need in our community to take home and provide for their families
rows.apply: See a volunteer to get registered at first visit

rows.service: Offers supplemental food, housing repairs and/or remediation, transportation assistance, housing move-in assistance, household goods assistance
rows.eligibility: Medicaid clients that meet health criteria
rows.apply: Call, visit the office, or apply online

rows.service: Offers a food pantry with canned and dried goods. Also offers a soup kitchen

rows.service: Offers labor and materials for minor home repairs for older adults whose homes are a risk to their health and safety. Repairs may include wheelchair ramps, grab bars, windows, and basic heating appliances
rows.eligibility: Age 60 and over with a home that is a threat to health and safety. Must be homeowners or renters with written landlord permission and meet income limits
rows.documents: Proof of income and proof of homeownership or written landlord permission for renters

rows.service: Offers door-to-door bus service both in and out of the county. Wheelchair accessible vans available on some routes
rows.eligibility: Open to all
rows.apply: In County Rides: Call by 2:00pm the day before to schedule a ride for the next day. Out of County Rides: Call at least 2 business days ahead to schedule a ride. These appointments can be scheduled for no earlier than 10:00am and no later than 1:00pm

rows.service: Offers rental properties with rent based on income
rows.eligibility: Meet income limits and pass a background check

rows.agency: DEPARTMENT OF SOCIAL SERVICES - CHEROKEE COUNTY
rows.service: Special Assistance offers a cash supplement to help pay for room and board in an adult care home or group home. Special Assistance In-Home offers a cash supplement to help people who are at risk of entering a residential facility but prefer to live at home. Long Term Care offers a cash supplement to help pay for room and board in a long term care or skilled nursing facility. Payments are made to the vendor
rows.eligibility: Meet income limits, be eligible for an approved residential or skilled nursing facility, and have a disability or be age 65 and over
rows.apply: Special Assistance Programs: Visit to complete an application or call to have it mailed. Long Term Care: Visit to complete an application, call to have it mailed, or apply online at epass.nc.gov (https://epass.nc.gov/)
rows.documents: Special Assistance Programs: Last 3 years of bank statements, proof of household income and resources, proof of monthly expenses, and the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household. Long Term Care: Last 5 years of bank statements, an FL-2 form from a doctor, proof of household income and resources, proof of monthly expenses, and the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household

rows.service: Delivers meals to homebound older adults
rows.eligibility: Homebound, age 60 and over and unable to prepare meals or attend a congregate lunch site

rows.agency: DEPARTMENT OF SOCIAL SERVICES - CHEROKEE COUNTY

rows.eligibility: Medicaid: Eligibility based on age, pregnancy status, disability status, income, and resources. Medicaid Transportation: People already receiving Medicaid through Medicaid Direct or the Eastern Band of Cherokee Indians tribal health plan. rows.apply: Medicaid: Visit to complete an application, call to have it mailed, or apply online at epass.nc.gov (https://epass.nc.gov/). Medicaid Transportation: Call to schedule medical transportation
rows.documents: Proof of household income and resources, proof of monthly expenses, and the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household

rows.agency: DEPARTMENT OF SOCIAL SERVICES - AVERY COUNTY
rows.service: Special Assistance offers a cash supplement to help pay for room and board in an adult care home or group home. rows.agency: DEPARTMENT OF SOCIAL SERVICES - AVERY COUNTY

rows.service: Offers financial help with heating bills once every 12 months. Payments are based on household size, income, and heating source. Payments are made to the vendor. People do not need to be in a crisis to get help

rows.apply: Visit or call to have an application mailed. NOTE: Households with a person age 60 and over or with a person under age 60 who is receiving services from the NC Division of Aging and Adult Services can apply beginning December 1. All other households can apply beginning January 1
rows.documents: Photo ID, birth certificate, proof of household income, copy of utility bill, latest bank statement, the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household, and proof of disability if under age 60

rows.service: Special Assistance offers a cash supplement to help pay for room and board in an adult care home or group home. Special Assistance In-Home offers a cash supplement to help people who are at risk of entering a residential facility but prefer to live at home. Long Term Care offers a cash supplement to help pay for room and board in a long term care or skilled nursing facility. Payments are made to the vendor
rows.eligibility: Meet income limits, be eligible for an approved residential or skilled nursing facility, and have a disability or be age 65 and over. Must be a US citizen or eligible alien
rows.apply: Special Assistance Programs: Visit to complete an application or call to have it mailed. Long Term Care: Visit to complete an application, call to have it mailed, or apply online at epass.nc.gov (https://epass.nc.gov/)
rows.documents: Special Assistance Programs: Last 3 years of bank statements, proof of household income and resources, proof of monthly expenses, and the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household. Long Term Care: Last 5 years of bank statements, an FL-2 form from a doctor, proof of household income and resources, proof of monthly expenses, and the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household

rows.agency: NORTH CAROLINA DEPARTMENT OF HEALTH AND HUMAN SERVICES
rows.service: What is Disaster Energy Assistance Disaster Energy Assistance is a temporary emergency program that helps eligible households and families afford energy heating services after a natural disaster or an emergency. The program offers help for eligible low-income households to offer, restore or continue heating sources. Disaster Energy Assistance is available until funds are exhausted. Who is eligible To be eligible for Disaster Energy Assistance, a household must have at least 1 US citizen or eligible non-citizen and: Have income equal to or less than 60% of the state median income Have resided in 1 of the identified 25 disaster declared counties listed during Hurricane Helene and are currently residing in 1 of those 25 counties Have been impacted and suffered a loss because of Hurricane Helene What does the program cover Utility bills, reconnection costs/fees, deposits, and meter repair Propane/fuel oil tanks Natural gas/propane line repair Propane/fuel oil delivery Insulation replacement Necessary wiring/circuit board replacement to be able to heat home Repair or replacement costs for furnaces. Coats and blankets, as tangible benefits to keep individuals warm. Space heaters/electric heaters. Individuals in 1 of the identified 25 disaster declared counties can apply in-person or via telephone with their l (https://www.ncdhhs.gov/localDSS)ocal county Department of Social Services. County Hours and Phone Number Alexander 828-632-1080 Monday - Friday, 8:00am - 5:00pm Alleghany 336-372-2411 Monday - Friday, 8:00am - 5:00pm Ashe 336-846-5719 Monday - Friday, 8:00am - 5:00pm Avery 828-733-8230 Monday - Friday, 8:00am - 4:30pm Buncombe 828-250-5500 Monday - Friday, 8:00am - 5:00pm Burke 828-764-9600 Monday - Friday, 8:00am - 5:00pm Caldwell 828-426-8200 Monday - Friday, 8:00am - 5:00pm Catawba 828-695-5600 Monday - Friday, 8:00am - 5:00pm Clay 828-389-6301 Monday - Friday, 8:00am - 5:00pm Cleveland 980-484-6000 Monday - Friday, 8:00am - 5:00pm Gaston 704-862-7500 Monday - Friday, 8:00am - 5:00pm Haywood 828-452-6
rows.eligibility: Must meet income limits and affected by Hurricane Helene. Household must have a US citizen or a non-citizen with qualified immigration status
rows.apply: Call or visit local Department of Social Services
rows.notes: Listed website covid19.ncdhhs.gov is now a general COVID-19 portal and does not address the Disaster Energy Assistance program; recommend phone confirmation via local DSS before referral

rows.service: Farmers markets offer fresh produce and other items sold by local farmers. For each dollar a SNAP user withdraws from their account to spend at the market, they will be given a token worth an additional dollar. People will need to contact or visit their individual farmers market to see if it is participating in this program. These facilities may have Special Care Units for people with Alzheimer's Disease or other related dementia. https://info.ncdhhs.gov/dhsr/nhlcs/pdf/achscubeds.pdf (https://info.ncdhhs.gov/dhsr/nhlcs/pdf/achscubeds.pdf)

rows.service: Regulates investor-owned agencies such as Duke Energy, Dominion Energy, PSNC Energy, and more. Helps customers make payment arrangements, offers information, and answers questions about billings. Investigates complaints by customers of the public utility companies. Rights of Utility Consumers: NOTE: The following rules only apply to utility companies that are regulated by the NC Utilities Commission - they do not apply to towns and membership cooperatives that provide utilities. Someone must be given a specific number of days to pay their bill. For example, electric and gas bills include a past-due date that is at least 25 days after the billing date. If the bill has not been paid by the past-due date, the company can charge a late fee of up to 1%. If payment is late, the utility company must provide a written notice at least 10 days before they disconnect service. The notice generally states that the amount that must be paid within 10 days to keep service from getting cut off. The company must make contact again at least 24 hours before they disconnect service. From November 1 through March 31, utility companies cannot disconnect service to households that: include someone who is disabled or age 65 or older; are unable to pay under an installment plan; and are certified by a local social services agency as eligible to receive assistance under an energy assistance program. Power cannot be disconnected on Fridays, weekends, state or federal holidays, or the day before a state or federal holiday. What companies are regulated by the NC Utilities Commission The following are examples of companies regulated by the NC Utilities Commission. The Commission does not regulate companies such as electric membership cooperatives or towns/municipalities. Duke Energy Progress Duke Energy Carolinas Dominion Energy/PSNC Energy Frontier Natural Gas Piedmont Natural Gas For more information, visit ncuc.gov/Consumer/whatwedo.html (https://www.ncuc.gov/Consumer/whatwedo.html). How does someone apply for the utility disconnection protection From November 1 through March 31, utility companies cannot disconnect service to households that meet the following requirements: have someone who is disabled or age 65 or older (or both) are unable to pay under an installment plan are certified by a local social services agency as eligible to receive assistance under an energy assistance program Duke Energy: People must get a "Certification Utilities Moratorium" form from the Department of Social Services. The form must be mailed to Duke Energy, 9700 David Taylor Drive, Charlotte, NC 28262. duke-energy.com/home/billing/special-assistance/moratorium (https://www.duke-energy.com/home/billing/special-assistance/moratorium) Dominion Energy/PSNC Energy No specific instructions. People should call 866-366-4357 for electric or 877-776-2427 for gas. Frontier Natural Gas: No specific instructions. People should call 336-526-2690. Piedmont Natural Gas: No specific instructions. People should call 800-752-7504
rows.eligibility: Customers of public utilities
rows.notes: Site confirms address (430 N Salisbury St, Dobbs Bldg 5th Floor, Raleigh 27603) but does not list hours or phone; 211 details retained.

rows.service: Offers financial help with past due rent, mortgage, and utilities. Limited appointments available. Does not help with hotels, car payments, internet or cell phone bills. Funding is limited
rows.eligibility: Age 18 and over, experiencing an unexpected financial crisis, and working at least 20 hours per week or receiving unemployment, Social Security, pension, or other documented income. Child support does not count as a source of income
rows.apply: Call Tuesday mornings beginning at 9:30am. You may not get through due to call volume. There is a call que and calls are answered upon the order received. Calls accepted until slots are filled. You may contact Friendship Fund or Doorstep Ministry they are a partnership that offers the same program, but are two different organizations. If you get through to one location, don't apply at the other organization. Appointments are first come-first served and fill up quickly
rows.documents: Photo ID, proof of income, lease/mortgage, and proof of past due bill
rows.notes: Agency website does not detail Doorstep Ministry/Friendship Fund rent assistance program; church main address 112 S Salisbury St; using 211 data — recommend phone confirmation

rows.agency: DEPARTMENT OF SOCIAL SERVICES - JOHNSTON COUNTY
rows.service: Special Assistance offers a cash supplement to help pay for room and board in an adult care home or group home. Special Assistance In-Home offers a cash supplement to help people who are at risk of entering a residential facility but prefer to live at home. Long Term Care offers a cash supplement to help pay for room and board in a long term care or skilled nursing facility. Payments are made to the vendor
rows.eligibility: Meet income limits, be eligible for an approved residential or skilled nursing facility, and have a disability or be age 65 and over. Must be a US citizen or eligible alien
rows.apply: Special Assistance Programs: Visit to complete an application or call to have it mailed. Long Term Care: Visit to complete an application, call to have it mailed, or apply online at epass.nc.gov (https://epass.nc.gov/)
rows.documents: Special Assistance: Last 3 years of bank statements, proof of household income and resources, proof of monthly expenses, and the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household. Long Term Care: Last 5 years of bank statements, an FL-2 form from a doctor, proof of household income and resources, proof of monthly expenses, and the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household
rows.notes: Site confirms 714 North Street, Smithfield 27577; (919) 989-5300; Mon-Fri 8am-5pm. Matches 211.

rows.agency: DEPARTMENT OF SOCIAL SERVICES - JOHNSTON COUNTY
rows.service: Offers health insurance for people with low income and limited resources. Medicaid transportation is available to Medicaid recipients who need assistance getting to Medicaid-approved medical appointments and providers
rows.eligibility: Medicaid: Eligibility based on age, pregnancy status, disability status, income, and resources. Must be a US citizen or eligible alien. Medicaid Transportation: People already receiving Medicaid through Medicaid Direct or the Eastern Band of Cherokee Indians tribal health plan. People already receiving Medicaid through a different health plan (AmeriHealth Caritas, Carolina Complete Health, HealthyBlue, UnitedHealthcare, or WellCare) must contact their plan provider for transportation
rows.apply: Medicaid: Visit to complete an application, call to have it mailed, or apply online at epass.nc.gov (https://epass.nc.gov/). Medicaid Transportation: Call to schedule medical transportation
rows.documents: Proof of household income and resources, proof of monthly expenses, and the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household
rows.notes: Site confirms 714 North Street, Smithfield 27577; (919) 989-5300; Mon-Fri 8am-5pm. Matches 211.

Other financial help may be available, based on sircumstances. Limited funds available
rows.eligibility: Crisis Intervention Program: Meet income limits and be at risk of a health-related emergency if utilities are shut off. May require a utility shut-off notice to be eligible. rows.agency: HOSPICE & PALLIATIVE CARE OF IREDELL COUNTY, INC.
rows.service: Provides registered nurses, certified nursing assistants, medical social workers, spiritual and bereavement counselors, and trained volunteers for patients with any life-limiting illness and support for their caregivers. This care is delivered directly to patients and their loved ones through intermittent visits. Nursing staff is available 24 hours a day, 7 days a week. In addition, the Gordon Hospice House, S. Mitchell Mack Hospice House, in-patient facility, provides around the clock care for acute hospice patients. Services: -In-home and inpatient hospice care -Palliative care -Bereavement support -Rainbow Kidz pediatric program- Offer a variety of bereavement programs providing care and support at home, in schools, and community to children 5 - 18 years of age. -Speakers bureau
rows.schedule: Monday - Friday, 8:00 a.m. - 5:00 p.m.; Nurses on call 24 hours, 7 days a week
rows.eligibility: Patient must have a terminal illness with a life-limiting prognosis
rows.apply: Anyone can make a referral by calling our office and then we will contact the physician to obtain approval for admission to the program
rows.notes: Site shows 2347 Simonton Road, Statesville (Statesville office); 211 listed 2341. Phone 704-873-4719 matches.

rows.documents: DD-214 or proof of service letter, VA Award Letter, and photo ID. Other documents will be emailed for applicants to sign within 7 business days
rows.notes: Purple Heart Homes site returned 403 on multiple paths; could not verify address or contact details.

rows.agency: DEPARTMENT OF SOCIAL SERVICES - IREDELL COUNTY
rows.service: Offers health insurance for people with low income and limited resources. Medicaid transportation is available to Medicaid recipients who need assistance getting to Medicaid-approved medical appointments and providers
rows.eligibility: Medicaid : Eligibility based on age, pregnancy status, disability status, income, and resources. Medicaid Transportation: People already receiving Medicaid through Medicaid Direct or the Eastern Band of Cherokee Indians tribal health plan. People already receiving Medicaid through a different health plan (AmeriHealth Caritas, Carolina Complete Health, HealthyBlue, UnitedHealthcare, or WellCare) must contact their plan provider for transportation
rows.apply: Medicaid : Visit to complete an application, call to have one mailed, or apply online at epass.nc.gov (https://epass.nc.gov/). Medicaid Transportation: Call to schedule medical transportation
rows.documents: Proof of household income and resources, proof of monthly expenses, and the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household

rows.agency: DEPARTMENT OF SOCIAL SERVICES - IREDELL COUNTY
rows.service: Offers temporary financial help for people in a crisis. Crisis Intervention Program (CIP) : Offers financial help with utility bills for heating or cooling emergencies. Payments are made to the vendor. Help is available more than once every 12 months. Other financial help may be available for rent, based on circumstances. Limited funds available
rows.eligibility: Crisis Intervention Program: Meet income limits and be at risk of a health-related emergency if utilities are shut off. May require a utility shut-off notice to be eligible. Requirements for other financial help may vary
rows.apply: Call to see if funds are available and to see if an application can be completed over the phone. Visit to complete an application or call to have 1 mailed
rows.documents: Proof of crisis, photo ID, birth certificate, proof of household income, copy of utility bill or payment receipt, and the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household

rows.service: Offers financial help with rent and utility bills
rows.eligibility: Residents of Iredell County except residents of the city of Mooresville
rows.documents: Photo ID and copy of bill or proof of rent. Max assistance is $100
rows.notes: Site shows Mon-Wed 8:30am-2pm, Thu 8:30am-3:30pm; Friday hours vary. Different from 211's narrower windows.

rows.service: Offers emergency financial help with rent and utilities. Not able to help with deposits. Payment goes directly to the vendor. Limited funding available
rows.eligibility: Meet income limits and be in a crisis situation. Financial help can only be given once every 12 months. Priority is given to homes with minors or people with health conditions
rows.documents: Help with Utilities: Photo ID, proof of household income, copies of monthly bills or receipts, and a final disconnect/shut-off notice. Help with Rent: Photo ID, proof of household income, copies of monthly bills or receipts, eviction notice, and a completed Salvation Army Rental Assistance Form
rows.notes: Site redirects to salvationarmyusa.org/nc/statesville/caldwell-st-corps; corps hours Mon-Thu 9-4 match 211; rent assistance specifics not detailed on site.

rows.service: Offers vouchers for approved Section 8 properties. Vouchers offer financial help with rent

rows.apply: Call or visit Waitlist Check (https://www.waitlistcheck.com/NC1996) for waitlist information
rows.documents: Photo ID, Social Security card, birth certificate, and proof of income

rows.eligibility: Open to all

rows.agency: DEPARTMENT OF SOCIAL SERVICES - IREDELL COUNTY
rows.service: Special Assistance offers a cash supplement to help pay for room and board in an adult care home or group home. Special Assistance In-Home offers a cash supplement to help people who are at risk of entering a residential facility but prefer to live at home. Long Term Care offers a cash supplement to help pay for room and board in a long term care or skilled nursing facility. Payments are made to the vendor
rows.eligibility: Meet income limits, live in an approved residential or skilled nursing facility, and have a disability or be age 65 and over. Must be a US citizen or eligible alien
rows.apply: Special Assistance: Visit to complete an application or call to have one mailed. Long Term Care: Visit to complete an application, call to have one mailed, or apply online at epass.nc.gov (https://epass.nc.gov/)
rows.documents: Special Assistance: Last 3 years of bank statements, proof of household income and resources, proof of monthly expenses, and the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household. Long Term Care: Last 5 years of bank statements, an FL-2 form from a doctor, proof of household income and resources, proof of monthly expenses, and the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household

rows.agency: DEPARTMENT OF SOCIAL SERVICES - IREDELL COUNTY
rows.service: Offers financial help with heating bills once every 12 months. Payments are based on household size, income, and heating source. Payments are made to the utility provider. People do not need to be in a crisis to get help
rows.eligibility: Meet income limits and resource guidelines. Each household is only eligible once, even if multiple members apply
rows.apply: Call to see if funds are available. Visit to complete an application, call to have one mailed, or apply online at epass.nc.gov (https://epass.nc.gov/). NOTE: Households with a person age 60 and over or with a person under age 60 who is receiving services from the NC Division of Aging and Adult Services can apply beginning December 1. All other households can apply beginning January 1
rows.documents: Photo ID, birth certificate, proof of household income, copy of utility bill, latest bank statement, the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household, and proof of disability if under age 60

rows.service: Offers financial help with electric bills to qualifying people with high heating or cooling costs. Payment goes directly to the vendor
rows.eligibility: Customers of EnergyUnited EMC who meet income limits. High bill must be due to heating or cooling costs. Does not include propane customers
rows.documents: Completed application, proof of age or disability, and proof of household income (such as most recent tax returns or Social Security statement). Other documents may be needed. W2s are not accepted in place of tax returns

rows.service: Offers financial help with replacement or repair or HVAC unites, roofs, septic tanks, and handicap accessibility
rows.eligibility: Must live in the unincorporated areas of Edgecombe County (outside of a city or town) and meet income limits. Home must be owner-occupied, and must own the property and home. Priority is given to households that have never been served by Edgecombe County, people with disabilities, older adults, and veterans, single-parent households with a dependent, and income limits

rows.agency: DEPARTMENT OF SOCIAL SERVICES - EDGECOMBE COUNTY
rows.service: Special Assistance offers a cash supplement to help pay for room and board in an adult care home or group home. Special Assistance In-Home offers a cash supplement to help people who are at risk of entering a residential facility but prefer to live at home. Long Term Care offers a cash supplement to help pay for room and board in a long term care or skilled nursing facility. Payments are made to the vendor
rows.eligibility: Meet income limits, be eligible for an approved residential or skilled nursing facility, and have a disability or be age 65 and over. Must be a US citizen or eligible alien
rows.apply: Special Assistance: Visit to complete an application or call to have it mailed. Long Term Care: Visit to complete an application, call to have it mailed, or apply online at https://epass.nc.gov/ (https://epass.nc.gov/)
rows.documents: Special Assistance Programs: Last 3 years of bank statements, proof of household income and resources, proof of monthly expenses, and the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household. Long Term Care: Last 5 years of bank statements, an FL-2 form from a doctor, proof of household income and resources, proof of monthly expenses, and the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household
rows.notes: Site shows Tarboro office (122 E St James St) phone is 2526417611; 211 used Rocky Mount number.

rows.agency: DEPARTMENT OF SOCIAL SERVICES - EDGECOMBE COUNTY
rows.service: Offers health insurance for people with low income and limited resources. Medicaid transportation is available to Medicaid recipients who need assistance getting to Medicaid-approved medical appointments and providers
rows.eligibility: Medicaid: Eligibility based on age, pregnancy status, disability status, income, and resources. Must be a US citizen or eligible alien. Medicaid Transportation: People already receiving Medicaid through Medicaid Direct or the Eastern Band of Cherokee Indians tribal health plan. People already receiving Medicaid through a different health plan (AmeriHealth Caritas, Carolina Complete Health, HealthyBlue, UnitedHealthcare, or WellCare) must contact their plan provider for transportation
rows.apply: Medicaid : Visit to complete an application, call to have it mailed, or apply online at epass.nc.gov (https://epass.nc.gov/). Medicaid Transportation: Call to schedule medical transportation
rows.documents: Proof of household income and resources, proof of monthly expenses, and the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household
rows.notes: Site shows Tarboro office (122 E St James St) phone is 2526417611; 211 used Rocky Mount number.

rows.service: Offers a residential facility for people with intellectual disabilities and developmental disabilities. Services help people improve their self-help, daily living, and community living skills. Offers 24-hour on-call services, community integration, money management, medication monitoring, dining, housekeeping, and human rights and self-governance training
rows.eligibility: Adults, age 18 and over, who require active treatment services and have intellectual developmental disabilities or another closely related condition. Must have substantial delays in 3 or more major life function areas; self-care, mobility, communication, self-direction and the capacity to live independently
rows.documents: Completed application and psychological evaluation or other assessments, if available
rows.notes: Homepage confirmed phone 828-438-6026; specific Morganton address not on this page.

rows.eligibility: Medicaid: Eligibility based on age, pregnancy status, disability status, income, and resources. Must be a US citizen or eligible alien. rows.eligibility: Age 60 and over and not receiving Medicaid
rows.apply: Visit to complete application. If approved, request transportation at least 7 days before a ride is needed
rows.notes: src.nhcgov.com redirects; agency confirmed at 2222 South College Road.

rows.service: Offers curb-to-curb bus service
rows.schedule: Mon 7:00am - 7:00pm; Tue 7:00am - 7:00pm; Wed 7:00am - 7:00pm; Thu 7:00am - 7:00pm; Fri 7:00am - 7:00pm; Sat 9:00am - 5:00pm; Sun 9:00am - 5:00pm;
rows.eligibility: People with disabilities who are unable to use a fixed-route bus
rows.apply: Download the application online or call to have one mailed. Second part of application requires an assessment from a licensed medical professional. Return application in person or by mail. An interview will be scheduled. Once approved, call or email to schedule a ride
rows.notes: Site references DART Accessible Van Service; Castle Hayne address not detailed on homepage.

rows.service: Offers home mortgage, and mortgage refinancing loans to people unable to use traditional banking services
rows.eligibility: Open to all
rows.apply: Call, visit the office, or apply online
rows.documents: Photo ID and Social Security Card
rows.notes: Self-Help website returned 403; could not verify branch details, kept 211 values.

rows.service: A 31-home tiny home community of permanent supportive housing for people experiencing homelessness for over 1 year and have a disability
rows.eligibility: Must have been experiencing homelessness in Wilmington for over 1 year and be receiving government disability income
rows.documents: Completed application, copy of photo ID, income verification, and insurance verification. Pet and vehicle documentation, if applicable

rows.service: Offers permanent housing for women experiencing chronic homelessness
rows.eligibility: Women experiencing chronic homelessness. Must be at least 30 days drug and alcohol free, and in active recovery if there's history of substance use disorder
rows.apply: Call for Coordinated Entry intake and receive a referral or visit during walk in hours
rows.documents: A proof of homelessness letter from a caseworker, therapist, shelter staff, or other professional detailing your current living situation, on letterhead, signed and dated. If seeing a mental health professional, a copy of their latest clinical or psychological assessment. A copy of criminal history from each State, if applicable
rows.notes: Address confirmed; supportive housing listed; specific hours not posted.

rows.service: Offers permanent housing for people with disabilities that have been homeless for at least 12 months. Also offers transportation, counseling, life skills training, and more
rows.eligibility: People with disabilities who have been homeless for at least 12 months
rows.notes: Site confirms SECU Lakeside Reserve supportive housing program.

rows.service: Offers crisis intervention, shelter, support groups, and more for people experiencing domestic violence
rows.eligibility: Victims of domestic violence
rows.apply: Call, chat online, or visit the office

rows.service: Offers essential items, services, and guidance to children aging out of foster care
rows.schedule: Appointment only. Call for more information
rows.eligibility: Youth age 14 - 17, and young adults age 18 - 26
rows.apply: Visit website to fill out application
rows.documents: Verification of foster care status with DHHS

rows.service: A long-term homeless shelter for men. Offers food, clothing, and self-help groups for shelter residents
rows.eligibility: Men, age 18 and over, experiencing homelessness, able to work and lift 50 pounds
rows.apply: Call during office hours to schedule an intake appointment
rows.notes: Website confirms general info but does not detail shelter program specifics; keeping 211 data.

rows.service: Offers bus service, with schedules that vary by route. Find real-time route information by calling 910-343-0106, or visiting http://tracking.wavetransit.com/ (http://tracking.wavetransit.com/)
rows.schedule: Mon 7:00am - 7:00pm; Tue 7:00am - 7:00pm; Wed 7:00am - 7:00pm; Thu 7:00am - 7:00pm; Fri 7:00am - 7:00pm; Sat 9:00am - 5:00pm; Sun 9:00am00am - 5:00pm;
rows.eligibility: Open to all

rows.service: Offering emergency financial help with rent, utilities, medical, and transportation needs. Unable to help with hotel fees
rows.eligibility: Must have eviction or disconnection notice for help with rent or utilities. Provides financial help to individuals once a year

rows.documents: Completed intake form, photo ID, lease agreement, delinquent utility bill, and proof of income. Other documents may be needed, depending on service
rows.notes: Site confirms Help Hub program for emergency financial assistance, housing navigation, and healthcare assistance.

rows.service: Offers an on-demand ride sharing program. Does not require pre-booking and does not rely on a fixed-route
rows.schedule: Regional Zones 1 and 2 Monday - Friday, 6:30am - 10:00am, 12:00pm - 7:00pm. Local Zones 3 and 4 Monday - Friday, 6:00am - 8:00pm, Saturday, 8:00am - 6:00pm, Sunday 9:00am - 5:00pm
rows.eligibility: Open to all
rows.apply: Call or schedule a ride on website
rows.notes: Site references RideMICRO; specific zone hours not detailed on homepage.

rows.service: Offers vouchers for approved Section 8 properties. Vouchers offer financial help with rent
rows.eligibility: Meet income limits and pass a background check
rows.documents: Photo ID, Social Security card, birth certificate, and proof of income

rows.agency: FAMILY PROMISE OF THE LOWER CAPE FEAR
rows.service: Offers an emergency shelter for families experiencing homelessness. Also offers case management, meals, laundry and showers
rows.eligibility: Families experiencing homelessness with children age 17 and under

rows.documents: Photo ID, Social Security card for all household members, child's birth certificate and immunization records
rows.notes: familypromiselcf.org redirects to familypromiselowercapefearnc.org; address and phone confirmed.

rows.service: Offers referrals to homeless shelters in the area. People DO NOT have to go through Coordinated Entry to get access to a shelter but it is an option that can offer help
rows.eligibility: People experiencing homelessness or at-risk of homelessness
rows.notes: NCCEH provides referrals; specific Wilmington coordinated entry contact not detailed on main resources page.

rows.service: Offers a lunch meal
rows.eligibility: Open to all

rows.service: Local volunteers work with eligible families to build homes. Qualified homebuyers pay back a mortgage over 30 years
rows.eligibility: Meet income limits, have lived in the area for the last 12 months, and are willing to contribute "sweat equity" volunteer hours. Some people may be eligible if they have only worked in the area for the last 12 months, rather than lived in the area. Must have ability to pay a monthly mortgage, and pass a credit and criminal background check
rows.documents: Proof of household income and address

rows.service: Helps people living in an institution explore options for moving back into the community. Offers housing support to people with disabilities to remain independent in their community and prevent homelessness. Also helps homeless people with disabilities in their search for housing
rows.eligibility: People with disabilities who are at risk of entering an institution or experiencing homelessness. People who are already in an institution but want to move home
rows.apply: Call or visit website for more information

rows.service: Offers a transitional housing program for adult women who are victims of human trafficking. This service is limited
rows.eligibility: Human trafficking victims as defined by The Victims Protection Act: exploited for labor, services, or commercial sex. On-campus housing for single women age 18 and older
rows.apply: Call the Coordinated Entry Hotline for intake, applications cannot be accepted until a CE referral is sent to the agency
rows.documents: A proof of homelessness letter from a caseworker, therapist, shelter staff, or other professional detailing your current living situation, on letterhead, signed and dated. If seeing a mental health professional, a copy of their latest clinical or psychological assessment. A copy of criminal history from each State, if applicable
rows.notes: Site confirms trafficking survivor housing; specific hours not posted.

rows.service: Offers short-term housing for 90 days or less for veterans experiencing homelessness. Offers meals, clothing, toiletries and laundry supplies, transportation to medical appointments, and more
rows.eligibility: Veterans experiencing homelessness
rows.apply: Call to schedule an in-person intake
rows.documents: Proof of income and verification of homelessness

rows.service: Offers transitional housing, food, and clothing for formerly incarcerated and recently released from incarceration
rows.eligibility: Men and women, age 25 and over, recently released or being released from incarceration, and on probation or parole. Must call probation or parole officer for approval before contacting agency. There is one campus location in Wilmington
rows.notes: Site confirms M.E.R. Transitional Living Campus at 222 Division Drive and phone 910-332-1132.

rows.agency: DEPARTMENT OF SOCIAL SERVICES - NEW HANOVER COUNTY
rows.service: Offers temporary financial help for people in a crisis. Crisis Intervention Program (CIP): Offers financial help with utility bills for heating or cooling emergencies. Payments are made to the vendor. Help is available more than once every 12 months
rows.eligibility: Meet income limits and be at risk of a health-related emergency if utilities are shut off. May require a utility shut-off notice to be eligible
rows.documents: Proof of crisis, photo ID, birth certificate, proof of household income, copy of utility bill or payment receipt, and the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household
rows.notes: Site redirects to nhcgov.com/417/Social-Services/.

rows.service: Offers financial help for older adults to pay for electric or water bills
rows.schedule: Mon 8:00am - 5:00pm; Tue 8:00am - 5:00pm; Wed 8:00am - 5:00pm; Thu 8:00am - 5:00pm; Fri 8:00am - 5:00pm; Sat 8:00am; Sun 8:00am;
rows.eligibility: Age 60 and over. Must have the funds to pay a part of the past-due bill. The agency will only pay the last $50 remaining on the electric or water bill. Help is available once every 12 months
rows.documents: Must have final cut-off notice for either water or electric bills. Applicant's name must be on the bill
rows.notes: src.nhcgov.com redirects to senior resource center page; specific utility assistance program details not separately listed.

rows.service: Offers water service
rows.eligibility: Residents within the service area
rows.apply: Call or visit website to confirm address is eligible for service. Apply online or walk in for help applying

rows.service: Offers services and activities for older adults. Offers exercise classes, games, and day and overnight trips. Offers seasonal/holiday parties, health education programs, and more. Offers information and referrals to community resources. Offers door-to-door transportation services for medical appointments, shopping, and more. Offers a computer lab. Also offers computer classes to help people learn to use computers and search the internet. May offer a computer upon completion of the computer class. Also offers a congregate lunch meal for older adults
rows.eligibility: Age 55 and over for most services. Age 60 and over for congregate meals
rows.notes: Homepage did not display contact details directly; senior center confirmed.

rows.service: Offers a food pantry where people can select a 3-day supply of food. Special dietary needs are considered whenever possible such as religious guidelines, diabetic limitations, allergies, and more
rows.eligibility: Wilson County residents, ONLY. Receive food stamps, EBT benefits, or meet income limits

rows.service: Offers clothing, food, and shelter

rows.service: Offers home repairs. Repairs are completed by construction students and volunteers under the supervision of a contractor. Also offers ramps for homeowners in need

rows.service: Offers support to unpaid, informal caregivers. Offers counseling, support groups, and caregiver training. Offers information and referrals to other community resources. Offers respite care to give caregivers a break from caregiving duties. Limited supplies of liquid nutritional supplements may be available
rows.eligibility: Unpaid caregivers caring for someone age 60 and over or for someone of any age with Alzheimer's or related brain disorder. Also available for caregivers age 55 and over who are caring for a non-child relative under age 18 or for an adult with a disability
rows.notes: Site returned 403; could not verify. 211 info retained.

rows.agency: DEPARTMENT OF SOCIAL SERVICES - WILSON COUNTY
rows.service: Special Assistance offers a cash supplement to help pay for room and board in an adult care home or group home. Special Assistance In-Home offers a cash supplement to help people who are at risk of entering a residential facility but prefer to live at home. Long Term Care offers a cash supplement to help pay for room and board in a long term care or skilled nursing facility. Payments are made to the vendor
rows.eligibility: Meet income limits, be eligible for an approved residential or skilled nursing facility, and have a disability or be age 65 and over. Must be a US citizen or eligible alien
rows.apply: Special Assistance Programs: Visit to complete an application or call to have it mailed. Long Term Care: Visit to complete an application, call to have it mailed, or apply online at epass.nc.gov (https://epass.nc.gov/)
rows.documents: Special Assistance Programs: Last 3 years of bank statements, proof of household income and resources, proof of monthly expenses, and the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household. Long Term Care: Last 5 years of bank statements, an FL-2 form from a doctor, proof of household income and resources, proof of monthly expenses, and the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household
rows.notes: Wilson County NC government site returned 403; could not verify. 211 info retained.

rows.service: Delivers meals to homebound older adults. Also offers a congregate lunch meal for older adults. Transportation is available to and from the lunch sites
rows.schedule: Meals are served and delivered Monday - Friday, 9:00am - 12:00pm
rows.eligibility: Congregate Meals: Age 60 and over, or under 60 with a documented disability. Home Delivered Meals: Homebound, age 60 and over or under age 60 with a documented disability, and unable to prepare meals or attend a congregate lunch site
rows.apply: Call for more information or to request referral form. Doctors referral is required for Home Delivered Meals. Visit website to download referral and have doctor return completed form by fax
rows.documents: Doctor's referral for home delivered meals, with documentation of disability for those under the age of 60
rows.notes: Site lists Suite C at home office; phone 252-237-1303 confirmed.

rows.agency: DEPARTMENT OF SOCIAL SERVICES - WILSON COUNTY
rows.service: Offers health insurance for people with low income and limited resources. Medicaid transportation is available to Medicaid recipients who need assistance getting to Medicaid-approved medical appointments and providers
rows.eligibility: Medicaid: Eligibility based on age, pregnancy status, disability status, income, and resources. Must be a US citizen or eligible alien. Medicaid Transportation: People already receiving Medicaid through Medicaid Direct or the Eastern Band of Cherokee Indians tribal health plan. People already receiving Medicaid through a different health plan (AmeriHealth Caritas, Carolina Complete Health, HealthyBlue, UnitedHealthcare, or WellCare) must contact their plan provider for transportation
rows.apply: Medicaid: Visit to complete an application, call to have it mailed, or apply online at epass.nc.gov (https://epass.nc.gov/). Medicaid Transportation: Call to schedule medical transportation
rows.documents: Proof of household income and resources, proof of monthly expenses, and the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household
rows.notes: Wilson County NC government site returned 403 on multiple attempts; could not verify. 211 info retained.

rows.service: Offers home mortgage, and mortgage refinancing loans to people unable to use traditional banking services

rows.notes: Site returned 403 on fetch attempts. Could not verify; keeping 211 values.

rows.service: Offers rapid rehousing services. Funds can be used for a security deposit, first month's rent, and utility deposit

rows.service: Offers curb-to-curb bus service

rows.service: Local volunteers work with eligible families to build homes. Qualified homebuyers pay back a mortgage over 30 years

rows.agency: DEPARTMENT OF SOCIAL SERVICES - WILSON COUNTY
rows.service: Offers financial help with heating bills once every 12 months. Payments are based on household size, income, and heating source. Payments are made to the vendor. People do not need to be in a crisis to get help

rows.apply: Call to see if funds are available. Visit to complete an application, call to have 1 mailed, or apply online at epass.nc.gov (https://epass.nc.gov/). NOTE: Households with a person age 60 and over or a person with a documented disability can apply beginning December 1. Other households can apply beginning January 1
rows.documents: Photo ID, birth certificate, proof of household income, copy of utility bill, latest bank statement, the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household, and proof of disability if under age 60

rows.eligibility: Medicaid : Eligibility based on age, pregnancy status, disability status, income, and resources. Must be a US citizen or eligible alien. rows.service: Offersday and night shelter for men and women experiencing homelessness. Day shelter offers laundry and showers. Limited food and no kitchen on-site

rows.apply: Visit. Call to check availability of beds

rows.service: Offers bus service, with schedules that vary by route. Find real-time route information by calling or visiting www.nextbus.com/#!/winston-salem (http://www.nextbus.com/#!/winston-salem)

rows.service: Offers financial help with rent, mortgage, and utilities. Offers rent and utility deposits. Limited funds available
rows.schedule: Kernersville Office: Monday, Wednesday, Friday, 9:00am - 12:00pm Tuesday, Thursday 9:00am - 4:00pm. Winston-Salem Office: Monday - Friday, 9:00am - 4:00pm Closed for lunch 12:00pm - 1:00pm. Registration for interviews ends 45 minutes before the end of operating hours
rows.eligibility: Meet income limits and be in crisis. Examples of crisis may include: job loss, reduced work hours by an employer, reduced work hours due to illness, recent injury causing disability, car repair, housing condemned by the City or County, housing sold by the landlord, domestic abuse, loss of money due to theft verifiable by a police report, or increased medical expenses. People in the zip codes 27107 and 27127 must first go to Sunnyside Ministry for help
rows.apply: Call. Available on a first-come, first-served basis
rows.documents: Photo ID, proof of income, proof of expenses, proof of crisis, and bill information for rent, mortgage, or utility
rows.notes: Site shows Winston-Salem office (200 E 10th St) phone is 336-724-7453; 211 had Kernersville number.

rows.agency: DEPARTMENT OF SOCIAL SERVICES - FORSYTH COUNTY
rows.service: Offers financial help with rent
rows.eligibility: Meet income limits and be in a crisis. Must have a child under age 18 in the household
rows.apply: Call to see if funds are available and to see if an application can be completed over the phone
rows.documents: Proof of crisis, photo ID, birth certificate, proof of household income, copy of lease, and the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household
rows.notes: Forsyth County DSS website blocked from fetch; could not verify against 211 fields.

rows.service: Helps formerly incarcerated men and women find and keep permanent housing. Offers financial help with past due rent and utilities, and rental deposit. The maximum amount available is $300. Minor traffic violations do not qualify. Does NOT offer transitional housing, hotel, or rooming house
rows.apply: Apply online at www.eurekahouse.org/eurekahousingprogram (https://www.eurekahouse.org/new-page-3) and email required documents within 5 days of completing online application

rows.service: Offers emergency financial help with utilities, rent, mortgage, transportation, and more. Not able to help with deposits. Payment goes directly to the vendor. Funding is limited and separated by county. It is possible that some counties will have funding while other counties might run out. May offer household goods for people involved in natural disasters or house fires
rows.eligibility: Depends on individual situations. Call for more information. Clients must call to be pre-screened before an appointment is scheduled for help. During the pre-screen interview, case workers will verify the cause of crisis and review the client's income and expenses
rows.apply: Call to see if funding is available and to pre-screen for eligibility
rows.notes: Site confirms financial assistance at 1255 N Trade St; pre-screen schedule not posted online.

rows.service: Offers services to veterans who are at risk of homelessness, homeless veterans and their families. Offers outreach, case management, and help getting VA and other public benefits. Also offers short-term financial help with rent and move-in costs
rows.eligibility: Veterans who meet income limits, experiencing homelessness, at risk of homelessness, or their families
rows.notes: Site main contact phone is 336-723-3601; 211 had 336-978-0267. Site does not directly describe SSVF program; refers users to 211.

rows.service: Offers minor home repairs, including installing safety features. Offers in-home visits with homebound adults and limited respite. Also offers transportation for medical appointments and to the grocery store
rows.eligibility: Age 60 and over. Must live in Forsyth County. Be the homeowner for services involving minor home repairs or get permission from the landlord
rows.apply: Call. Some programs may have a waitlist
rows.documents: Proof of homeownership for home repairs or permission from landlord

rows.service: Offers curb-to-curb bus service
rows.eligibility: People with disabilities who are unable to use a fixed-route bus or age 60 and over. Stop must be located within 3/4 of a mile of a fixed-route bus stop

rows.notes: Site shows weekday hours 5:00am-11:00pm; 211 listed 5:30am-11:30pm.

rows.service: Offers referrals to homeless shelters in the area. People DO NOT have to go through Coordinated Entry to get access to a shelter but it is an option that can offer help

rows.service: Local volunteers work with eligible families to build homes. Qualified homebuyers pay back a mortgage over 30 years
rows.schedule: Mon 10:00am - 4:00pm; Tue 10:00am - 4:00pm; Wed 10:00am - 4:00pm; Thu 10:00am - 4:00pm; Fri by appointment only;
rows.eligibility: Meet income limits, have lived in the area for the last 12 months, and are willing to contribute "sweat equity" volunteer hours. Some people may be eligible if they have only worked in the area for the last 12 months, rather than lived in the area. Must have ability to pay a monthly mortgage, and pass a credit and criminal background check
rows.documents: Proof of household income, address, and rental history
rows.notes: Site shows M-Th 10-4 plus Fri by appointment; 211 omitted Friday.

rows.service: Offers transitional housing and support services for men formerly incarcerated, male veterans experiencing homelessness, and men experiencing homelessness. Offers job training, mental health services, and case management to participants
rows.eligibility: Men experiencing homelessness
rows.apply: Call, email, or visit the website to request an application. Completed applications can be submitted in person, via email or online
rows.documents: Photo ID, Social Security card, proof of homelessness or incarceration (letter or documentation from a shelter, outreach program, or caseworker verifying homelessness), and for Veterans a DD-214 form or other documentation verifying military service. Income verification (recent pay stubs, benefit statements, or proof of income). If unemployed, a statement from the unemployment office or other supporting documentation. Documents for Social Security Disability Insurance (SSDI), Supplemental Security Income (SSI), or Veterans Affairs (VA) benefits, if receiving assistance. Documentation of any disabilities or ongoing medical conditions and prescription history or medical evaluation (for participants requiring healthcare or disability services). Previous rental agreements or a letter from previous landlords, if available. Consent form for a background check (required for all applicants)

rows.service: Offers transitional housing and case management for male veterans experiencing homelessness

rows.documents: DD-214 form and proof of ID which can be a photo ID, an identification card, or a Social Security card

rows.agency: DEPARTMENT OF SOCIAL SERVICES - YADKIN COUNTY
rows.service: Offers financial help with heating bills once every 12 months. Payments are based on household size, income, and heating source. Payments are made to the utility provider. People do not need to be in a crisis to get help

rows.agency: DEPARTMENT OF SOCIAL SERVICES - WAKE COUNTY
rows.service: Special Assistance offers a cash supplement to help pay for room and board in an adult care home or group home. Special Assistance In-Home offers a cash supplement to help people who are at risk of entering a residential facility but prefer to live at home. Long Term Care offers a cash supplement to help pay for room and board in a long term care or skilled nursing facility. Payments are made to the vendor
rows.eligibility: Meet income limits, live in an approved residential or skilled nursing facility, and have a disability or be age 65 and over
rows.apply: Special Assistance Programs: Visit to complete an application or call to have it mailed. Long Term Care: Visit to complete an application, call to have it mailed, or apply online at epass.nc.gov (https://epass.nc.gov/)
rows.documents: Special Assistance Programs: Last 3 years of bank statements, proof of household income and resources, proof of monthly expenses, and the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household. Long Term Care: Last 5 years of bank statements, an FL-2 form from a doctor, proof of household income and resources, proof of monthly expenses, and the names and Social Security numbers for everyone in the household
rows.notes: wakegov.com redirects to wake.gov; main DSS line 919-212-7000 and Mon-Fri 8am-5:15pm hours confirmed.
